elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 1101.1: Introduction to the Guide (12/17/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Guide segments
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Guide series and topics
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Guide chapters and sections
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Numbering format
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Exhibits, forms, the Glossary and directories
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Guide consists of Freddie Mac’s requirements relating to the purchase, sale and Servicing of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages. The Guide’s structure reflects how and when Seller/Servicers interact with Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac and is organized to aid in locating the provisions related to the Seller/Servicer’s daily
elements.label: text

elements.text: workflow.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Guide segments
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Guide is grouped into three segments:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac Seller/Servicer Relationship
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Selling
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Guide’s design places the provisions applicable to all Seller/Servicers in one central
elements.label: text

elements.text: location (Freddie Mac Seller/Servicer Relationship) and then differentiates between those for
elements.label: text

elements.text: selling and Servicing. Although the Guide has distinct Selling and Servicing segments, there
elements.label: text

elements.text: may be information and/or cross references for Servicers in the Selling segment and for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sellers in the Servicing segment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additionally, the Guide contains exhibits, forms, the Glossary and directories.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Guide series and topics
elements.label: text

elements.text: Within each segment, there are three distinct series of topics relating to that segment, totaling
elements.label: text

elements.text: nine series. The titles of topics are intended to facilitate navigation and searching capabilities.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The series of topics within each segment are described below.

elements.text: (c) Guide chapters and sections
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each topic contains at least one chapter, and each chapter contains at least one section. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: titles of chapters and sections are intended to facilitate navigation and searching capabilities.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Numbering format
elements.label: text

elements.text: The numbering system of the Guide reflects how the provisions are grouped by segment,
elements.label: text

elements.text: series and topic.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The format is as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ XXXX.XX – The first digit indicates the series number (For example, Series 6000,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Selling and Delivery, or Series 5000, Origination and Underwriting)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ XXXX.XX – The second digit indicates the topic number, which can have one or many
elements.label: text

elements.text: chapters
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ XXXX.XX – The last 2 digits before the decimal point indicate the chapter number
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ XXXX.XX – The 2 digits after the decimal indicates the section number
elements.label: text

elements.text: For example, in Section 1101.1:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ 1101.1 – The first “1” represents that this section is located in Series 1000, General
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contract Terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ 1101.1 – The second “1” represents that this section is located in the first topic of Series
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1000, Topic 1100 – The Guide
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ 1101.1 – The “01” before the decimal point represents that this section is located in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: first chapter within Topic 1100, Chapter 1101, The Guide
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ 1101.1 – The “1” after the decimal point represents that this is the first section in Chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1101, Section 1101.1, Introduction to the Guide
elements.label: text

elements.text: This numbering can help in locating specific content. For example, these are the steps a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer could take if it wants to locate the Guide requirements about reporting fraud
elements.label: text

elements.text: and other Suspicious Activity:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ First, the segment should be identified. This is a requirement that applies to both Sellers
elements.label: text

elements.text: and Servicers and is contained within the Freddie Mac Seller/Servicer Relationship
elements.label: text

elements.text: Segment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Second, the series should be found. As this is part of risk management, the relevant series
elements.label: text

elements.text: is Series 3000, Risk Management and Remedies.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Next, the topic needs to be determined. These requirements are contained within topic
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3200: Fraud Prevention, Detection and Reporting; Reporting Other Suspicious Activity.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Finally, the chapter and section within the topic need to be located. In this case, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: chapter title mirrors the topic heading, Chapter 3201: Fraud Prevention, Detection and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Reporting; Reporting Other Suspicious Activity. The specific provisions about this
elements.label: text

elements.text: subject are contained in Section 3201.2: Fraud and other Suspicious Activity reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The numerical reference to this provision, then, is Section 3201.2.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Directories: The directories contain Freddie Mac contact information (i.e., addresses,
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: requests and documentation. Guide references to directories are indicated with a bolded
elements.label: text

elements.text: parenthetical (e.g., “(see Directory 5)”).

elements.text: (a) Status of the Guide as a contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Effect of the Guide and other Purchase Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Guide governs the business relationship between a Seller/Servicer and Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: relating to the sale of Mortgages to, and Servicing of such Mortgages for, Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each Seller/Servicer must complete and submit an Annual Certification Report (as
elements.label: text

elements.text: described in Section 2101.10) that certifies that the Seller/Servicer has access to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Guide and complies with all requirements of the Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In connection with the sale of a Mortgage or pool of Mortgages to Freddie Mac, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Agrees that each transaction is governed by the Guide, the applicable Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contract and all other Purchase Documents, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Makes all appliable representations and warranties to, and agreements and covenants
elements.label: text

elements.text: with, Freddie Mac as set forth in the Purchase Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: Regardless of the Servicing option selected upon a sale of Mortgages to Freddie Mac, a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer agrees to service all such Mortgages that the Seller/Servicer has sold to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac in accordance with the requirements set forth in the Seller/Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: As set forth in Section 7101.1(b) and the applicable Transfer of Servicing agreement,
elements.label: text

elements.text: each Transferee Servicer agrees to service all Mortgages related to a Transfer of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing in accordance with the requirements set forth in the Transferee Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: All of a Seller/Servicer’s obligations to service Mortgages for Freddie Mac constitute,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and must be performed pursuant to, the Servicing Contract, and the Servicing obligations
elements.label: text

elements.text: assumed pursuant to any contract to sell Mortgages to Freddie Mac are merged into and
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be performed pursuant to such Servicing Contract.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon the Settlement Date of the sale of a Mortgage (or group of Mortgages) to Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac, or the Effective Date of Transfer of any Transfer of Servicing, the Seller/Servicer or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transferee Servicer agrees that the Servicing Contract and related Servicing Contracts
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rights that are the subject of a Concurrent or Subsequent Transfer of Servicing, as well
elements.label: text

elements.text: as any proceeds to be paid to the Seller/Servicer as Transferor Servicer for such Transfer
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Servicing, are subject to the Lien as defined in Section 1101.2(c).
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller/Servicer acknowledges that Freddie Mac’s agreement to purchase Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: from the Seller/Servicer pursuant to any individual Purchase Contract is based upon the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer’s agreement that the Mortgages purchased will be serviced by such
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer pursuant to the Servicing Contract, which Servicing Contract and related
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Contract Rights may be assigned to another Servicer pursuant to a Transfer of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing, subject to obtaining Freddie Mac’s prior written approval as set forth in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 7101.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following summarizes the legal and accounting treatment of Servicing Contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rights, including their non-ownership and implications for contract breaches with
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Under no circumstance does a Seller/Servicer retain any legal ownership of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Contract or any related Servicing Contract Rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac acknowledges that a Seller/Servicer may recognize Servicing Contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rights as assets on its balance sheet in the form of “mortgage servicing rights” in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with generally accepted accounting principles (GAAP); however, such
elements.label: text

elements.text: recognition as under GAAP does not confer to a Seller/Servicer any legal ownership
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest in any Servicing Contract Rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller/Servicer agrees that any failure to service any Mortgage in accordance
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the terms of the Servicing Contract, or any breach of any of the Seller/Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligations under any aspect of the Servicing Contract, shall be deemed to constitute
elements.label: text

elements.text: a breach of the entire contract and shall entitle Freddie Mac to terminate all or a
elements.label: text

elements.text: portion of the Servicing Contract and any related Servicing Contract Rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The termination of a portion of the Servicing Contract shall not alter the unitary,
elements.label: text

elements.text: indivisible nature of the Servicing Contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Servicer that services Mortgages for Freddie Mac is not also the Seller of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages to Freddie Mac, the Servicer must agree to service Mortgages for Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: by separate agreement (e.g., Form 960, Agreement for Concurrent Transfer of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Single-Family Mortgages, or Electronic Agreement for Subsequent Transfer of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing of Single-Family Mortgages (STOS Agreement)), which incorporates the
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable Purchase Documents. In such case, the separate agreement shall be deemed to
elements.label: text

elements.text: be one of the “Purchase Documents” that constitute the Servicing Contract.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition, in certain cases, a Seller and/or Servicer who uses certain Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: services will, by virtue of the provisions of the Guide, be deemed to have agreed upon
elements.label: text

elements.text: certain terms and conditions related to such services and their use.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Amendments to the Guide
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may, in its sole discretion, amend or supplement the Guide from time to
elements.label: text

elements.text: time. Amendments to the Guide may be a paper Record or an Electronic Record, as those
elements.label: text

elements.text: terms are defined in Chapter 1401. The Guide may not be amended orally. Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: may amend the Guide by:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Publishing Bulletins (applicable to all Sellers/Servicers), or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Entering into a Purchase Contract or other written or Electronic agreement applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: only to the Seller/Servicer that is a party to the Purchase Contract or agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: Bulletins expressly amend, supplement, revise or terminate specific provisions of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Guide. An amendment, supplement, revision or termination of a provision in the Guide is
elements.label: text

elements.text: effective as of the date specified by Freddie Mac in the applicable Bulletin.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Purchase Contract or other written agreement or Electronic agreement amends or
elements.label: text

elements.text: supplements specific provisions of the Guide for purposes of such Purchase Contract or
elements.label: text

elements.text: other agreement, as applicable. Such amendments or supplements to the Guide are
elements.label: text

elements.text: effective as of the date specified in the Purchase Contract or other agreement. Refer to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following Guide provisions for additional information related to amendments to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Guide:
elements.label: text

elements.text: on AllRegs. Freddie Mac disclaims all liability associated with such use by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers, and Seller/Servicers agree that Freddie Mac shall have no liability
elements.label: text

elements.text: arising from such use by Seller/Servicers.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Bulletins are published on AllRegs and FreddieMac.com. A Seller/Servicer with an
elements.label: text

elements.text: AllRegs subscription may receive notice of Bulletins directly from AllRegs. If a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer does not receive notice of Bulletins through AllRegs, the Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must take the steps necessary to receive the applicable Freddie Mac Single-Family
elements.label: text

elements.text: Update e-mails, which will notify the Seller/Servicer of Bulletin publications.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers who do not engage with Single-Family Update e-mails—by opening or
elements.label: text

elements.text: clicking any content—at least once every six months will be removed from the subscriber
elements.label: text

elements.text: list and must re-engage, which could include opening an e-mail, clicking a link within an
elements.label: text

elements.text: e-mail or navigating to the Single-Family subscription center to update preferences to
elements.label: text

elements.text: resume receiving notifications.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: A Seller/Servicer’s failure to take the appropriate steps to receive notices of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Bulletins does not relieve the Seller/Servicer of its legal obligations to comply with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: terms of the Bulletins.

elements.text: (iv)Effective date
elements.label: text

elements.text: The effective date of each section of the Guide is located at the beginning of each section,
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the right of the section number and name.

elements.text: (b) Reliance upon the advice of counsel
elements.label: text

elements.text: By entering into a Purchase Contract or into the Servicing Contract with Freddie Mac (e.g.,
elements.label: text

elements.text: in connection with the sale of Mortgages to Freddie Mac or a Concurrent or Subsequent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transfer of Servicing), the Seller/Servicer acknowledges that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ It is not relying upon Freddie Mac or any employee, agent or representative thereof, in
elements.label: text

elements.text: making its decision to enter into the contract, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 1201: General Freddie Mac Policies
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 1201.1: Objective of Freddie Mac’s purchase programs
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Freddie Mac was created by Congress in 1970 under the Federal Home Loan Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Corporation Act (12 U.S.C. 1451 et. seq.).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s public mission is to provide liquidity, stability and affordability to the United
elements.label: text

elements.text: States housing market.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac does this primarily by purchasing residential Mortgages originated by mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: lenders. In most instances, Freddie Mac packages these Mortgages into mortgage-related
elements.label: text

elements.text: securities, which are guaranteed by Freddie Mac and sold in the global capital markets.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac also invests in Mortgages and mortgage-related securities. Freddie Mac does not
elements.label: text

elements.text: originate Mortgages or lend money directly to consumers.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac supports the United States housing market and the overall economy by:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Providing America’s families with access to mortgage funding at lower rates
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Helping distressed Borrowers keep their homes and avoid foreclosure, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Providing consistent liquidity to the multifamily mortgage market, which includes providing
elements.label: text

elements.text: financing for affordable rental housing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additionally, Freddie Mac works closely with FHFA and the broader industry to build a stronger
elements.label: text

elements.text: housing finance system for the nation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1201.2: Overview of Freddie Mac’s purchase programs
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Freddie Mac purchase programs provide for the purchase of conventional Home Mortgages on a
elements.label: text

elements.text: whole loan basis. Freddie Mac does not set a minimum loan amount for purchases and
elements.label: text

elements.text: encourages the Seller to make loans of any amount.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages under all purchase programs are purchased by Freddie Mac on a Required Net Yield
elements.label: text

elements.text: basis to Freddie Mac. Freddie Mac compensates Seller/Servicers for Servicing Mortgages in an
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount equal to the amount of interest payments received on the Mortgages that is over and
elements.label: text

elements.text: above the Required Net Yield and authorizes Seller/Servicers to retain such compensation out of
elements.label: text

elements.text: such interest payments.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Under no circumstance does a Seller/Servicer retain any legal ownership in any portion of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest due or received under a Mortgage that is owned by Freddie Mac. When a Mortgage is
elements.label: text

elements.text: purchased, the Seller must select a Minimum Servicing Spread in an amount that provides
elements.label: text

elements.text: adequate Servicing compensation to the Seller/Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1201.3: Characterization and nature of Mortgage
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: purchase transactions (12/17/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller and Freddie Mac agree that each and every Mortgage purchase and sale transaction
elements.label: text

elements.text: entered into under the Purchase Documents (including, without limitation, any purchase and sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: under the Cash, Guarantor or MultiLender Swap programs)—whether sold with or without
elements.label: text

elements.text: recourse—is expressly intended by the Seller and Freddie Mac:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ To be construed as the Seller’s sale, transfer, conveyance and delivery of all such Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ To be construed as Freddie Mac’s purchase and receipt of such Mortgages, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Not to be construed as the Seller’s pledge to secure a debt or any other obligation
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller and Freddie Mac intend for the sale, transfer, conveyance and delivery of all
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages to Freddie Mac by the Seller to be true, absolute and unconditional sales conveying
elements.label: text

elements.text: all of the Seller’s right, title and interest in and to the Mortgages and all proceeds from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller represents and warrants to, and covenants and agrees with Freddie Mac, that it does
elements.label: text

elements.text: not retain any rights or legal ownership interests in the Mortgages it sells to Freddie Mac or in
elements.label: text

elements.text: any payment due under or proceeds from such Mortgages, including, but not limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any portion of the interest due or received under such Mortgages; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicing Contract Rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac acknowledges that a Seller/Servicer may recognize Servicing Contract Rights as
elements.label: text

elements.text: assets on its balance sheet in the form of “mortgage servicing rights” in accordance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: generally accepted accounting principles (GAAP); however, such recognition as under GAAP
elements.label: text

elements.text: does not confer any legal ownership interest in any Servicing Contract Rights or the Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If despite the intent of the Seller and Freddie Mac, the Mortgages (or any of them) are
elements.label: text

elements.text: determined to be property of the Seller (i.e., the sale was not a true sale), Freddie Mac and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller agree that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Purchase Documents created a security agreement within the meaning of the Uniform
elements.label: text

elements.text: Commercial Code (UCC) in effect in the applicable State, conveying to Freddie Mac a
elements.label: text

elements.text: security interest in all of the Seller’s right, title and interest in and to the Mortgage and all
elements.label: text

elements.text: proceeds from the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac is the secured party under such security agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The possession by Freddie Mac or its designee (including, without limitation, any applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: Document Custodian) of the Notes (and any related documents) shall be deemed to be
elements.label: text

elements.text: possession by the secured party for purposes of perfecting the security interest pursuant to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: UCC
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller shall assist Freddie Mac with any reasonable actions necessary to ensure that
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac receives a perfected security interest of first priority under applicable law; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac will have all of the rights and remedies of a secured party and creditor under the
elements.label: text

elements.text: UCC and may execute and file UCC financing statements as reasonably necessary
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1201.4: Limitation on Mortgage purchases and
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: commitments (12/17/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Limitation on number and amount of Mortgage purchases and commitments
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Review of aggregate purchase amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Limitation on number and amount of Mortgage purchases and commitments
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac reserves the right to, at its sole discretion:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Limit the number and/or aggregate dollar amount of Mortgage commitments it will
elements.label: text

elements.text: accept from any Seller. Maximums are subject to change by Freddie Mac at any time
elements.label: text

elements.text: without prior notice or publication.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Establish the Maximum Annual Mortgage Purchase Amount that Freddie Mac will
elements.label: text

elements.text: purchase from a Seller in any calendar year. Freddie Mac will notify a Seller in writing if
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac has established a Maximum Annual Mortgage Purchase Amount for that
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller for that calendar year and will inform the Seller of the maximum amount for that
elements.label: text

elements.text: year.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: The Maximum Annual Mortgage Purchase Amount will not be adjusted by a
elements.label: text

elements.text: purchase tolerance, and the purchase tolerances in Section 6401.1 will not apply to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Maximum Annual Mortgage Purchase Amount.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Limit the number and/or aggregate dollar amount of Home Mortgages it will purchase in
elements.label: text

elements.text: any subdivision, tract, Condominium Project, Planned Unit Development (PUD) or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ground lease community
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Establish maximum Mortgage purchase amounts for time frames other than one year
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Establish maximum purchase amounts for specific types of Mortgages based on
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage characteristics
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Establish representative mix requirements such that Mortgages sold to Freddie Mac must
elements.label: text

elements.text: have similar loan characteristics when compared to Mortgages sold by the Seller to other
elements.label: text

elements.text: investors
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Establish minimum Mortgage purchase amounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Review of aggregate purchase amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac has established a Maximum Annual Mortgage Purchase Amount with respect
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the Seller, Freddie Mac will, after a commitment has been made, review the aggregate
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount of Mortgages purchased from the Seller to date in the current calendar year.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will notify the Seller if the contract commitment amount stated in the Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contract, when added to the aggregate amount of Mortgage purchases to date from the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: for that calendar year, would cause the Seller to exceed the Maximum Annual Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Amount established for the Seller.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In such cases, Freddie Mac, in its sole and absolute discretion, may either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Purchase Mortgages from the Seller in an amount that exceeds the Seller’s Maximum
elements.label: text

elements.text: Annual Mortgage Purchase Amount; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Rescind, in whole or in part, any Purchase Contract (including any Purchase Contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: entered into by any other Freddie Mac authorized method) that will result in the Seller’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: exceeding its Maximum Annual Mortgage Purchase Amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller agrees that any such rescission shall be made without the payment by Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: of any Seller claims.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1201.5: Servicing in accordance with Purchase
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Documents (12/17/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages purchased by Freddie Mac must be serviced by a Servicer in accordance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable law and the applicable Purchase Documents. For performing Servicing duties, Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac will compensate the Servicer in accordance with Chapter 8103.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac reserves the right to refuse to purchase any Mortgage that, in its sole discretion,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac determines cannot be adequately serviced by the Seller (if also a Servicer), its
elements.label: text

elements.text: assignee or its duly authorized Servicing Agent.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1201.6: Transfer costs (12/17/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The Seller agrees to pay documentary stamp taxes, recording fees, transfer taxes and all other
elements.label: text

elements.text: expenses payable in connection with Mortgages purchased by Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1201.7: Sale of Mortgages by Freddie Mac (12/17/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Freddie Mac may, from time to time, sell in whole or in part Mortgages it has purchased
elements.label: text

elements.text: pursuant to the Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: For information regarding a termination of the Servicing Contract and related Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Contract Rights related to the sale of Mortgages by Freddie Mac, refer to Section 3603.1(b).
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1201.8: Receipt and treatment of confidential
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: information (12/17/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Receipt and treatment of Freddie Mac confidential information
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Treatment of Seller/Servicer confidential information
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Receipt and treatment of Freddie Mac confidential information
elements.label: text

elements.text: In connection with its relationship with Freddie Mac, Seller/Servicer may obtain, or Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac may provide Seller/Servicer with, information and documentation that Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: identifies or has identified as confidential (collectively, “Freddie Mac confidential
elements.label: text

elements.text: information”). Freddie Mac confidential information may include, but is not limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Information and documentation concerning the development, negotiation, operation or
elements.label: text

elements.text: terms of various products or programs
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Technology, business terms, trade secrets and commercial and financial information
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Confidential information may include confidential information belonging to third parties
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition, information that Seller/Servicer is required by applicable law to treat as
elements.label: text

elements.text: confidential and information that Seller/Servicer knows or should know should be treated as
elements.label: text

elements.text: confidential are considered Freddie Mac confidential information, whether or not Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac has identified the information as confidential. If a Seller/Servicer requests that Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac provide Freddie Mac confidential information to a third party, all Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: confidential information disclosed to the third party at Seller/Servicer’s request is Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac confidential information and will be deemed received by Seller/Servicer upon disclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the third party.
elements.label: text

elements.text: All information and materials that are based on, or include, Freddie Mac confidential
elements.label: text

elements.text: information (“derivative information”), including, without limitation, information and
elements.label: text

elements.text: materials created by Seller/Servicer, are also considered Freddie Mac confidential
elements.label: text

elements.text: information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Treatment of Freddie Mac confidential information
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer must treat all Freddie Mac confidential information as strictly
elements.label: text

elements.text: confidential and proprietary to Freddie Mac. The Seller/Servicer must not release or
elements.label: text

elements.text: disclose or permit the release or disclosure of Freddie Mac confidential information or
elements.label: text

elements.text: any portion thereof, for any purpose at any time except to the following extent (the
elements.label: text

elements.text: “Permitted Purposes”):
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Allowed by this Section 1201.8
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Expressly required or consented to by Freddie Mac in writing; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ordered by a court or administrative agency of competent jurisdiction
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller/Servicer anticipates that it may be required, for any reason, to release or
elements.label: text

elements.text: disclose Freddie Mac confidential information (other than as allowed in the first two
elements.label: text

elements.text: bullets of the preceding sentence), the Seller/Servicer shall immediately notify Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac (see Directory 1), and provide reasonable cooperation to Freddie Mac, to allow
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac to take any actions it deems necessary to prevent or limit the release or
elements.label: text

elements.text: disclosure of the Freddie Mac confidential information in question.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Copies of Freddie Mac confidential information
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer shall not copy or permit copies to be made of the Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: confidential information or any portion thereof, except to the extent necessary for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Permitted Purposes. The Seller/Servicer shall mark “Confidential” in a prominent
elements.label: text

elements.text: location on all Freddie Mac confidential information and copies.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Authorized parties
elements.label: text

elements.text: To the extent necessary to fulfill its Servicing obligations under the Servicing Contract or
elements.label: text

elements.text: other obligations to Freddie Mac (except as set forth below regarding the Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exclusionary List), the Seller/Servicer may provide Freddie Mac confidential information
elements.label: text

elements.text: and copies thereof, to the following persons (“Persons”):
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Officers, directors, principals, partners or employees of the Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller Servicer’s regulators, auditors, counsel and accountants
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any MI or other vendor
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any prospective Transferee Servicer, but only with respect to copies of a Transferor
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer’s Purchase Documents applicable to the Mortgages related to the Transfer of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing required to be provided pursuant to Section 7101.1(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer also may provide Freddie Mac confidential information and copies
elements.label: text

elements.text: thereof to Persons to the extent counsel for the Seller/Servicer determines that such
elements.label: text

elements.text: sharing is legally required. The Seller/Servicer must notify any Persons receiving the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac confidential information (and any copy thereof) that such Person has the
elements.label: text

elements.text: same obligations as the Seller/Servicer to keep the Freddie Mac confidential information
elements.label: text

elements.text: (and any copy thereof) confidential and may use it only for the Permitted Purpose for
elements.label: text

elements.text: which the information was provided to such Person.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer is responsible for compliance by all such Persons with the provisions
elements.label: text

elements.text: of this Section 1201.8. The Seller/Servicer will deliver to Freddie Mac a list of all such
elements.label: text

elements.text: Persons promptly upon request.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Under no circumstances may a Seller/Servicer provide or otherwise make available the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exclusionary List to any Person or entity (other than to the Seller/Servicer’s officer,
elements.label: text

elements.text: director, principal, partner or employee with a need to have the Exclusionary List for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: purposes set forth in this section) without the prior express written authorization of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Exclusions
elements.label: text

elements.text: Confidential information does not include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any information that is:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Generally available to the public without violation of the provisions of this Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Provided to the Seller/Servicer by a third party that is not itself under a
elements.label: text

elements.text: confidentiality obligation with respect to the information; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Independently developed by the Seller/Servicer without use of the Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: confidential information, derivative information or any portion thereof
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition, nothing in this Section 1201.8(a) will be deemed to preclude the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer from disclosing Freddie Mac confidential information to the extent
elements.label: text

elements.text: ordered by a court or agency of competent jurisdiction, as long as:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If legally permissible, the Seller/Servicer promptly notifies Freddie Mac of the order
elements.label: text

elements.text: and provides Freddie Mac with a reasonable opportunity to respond in such manner
elements.label: text

elements.text: as Freddie Mac deems appropriate to limit the disclosure; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller/Servicer notifies the court or agency in writing before any disclosure that
elements.label: text

elements.text: the information is Freddie Mac confidential information
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Treatment of Seller/Servicer confidential information
elements.label: text

elements.text: In response to Freddie Mac’s request, Seller/Servicer may provide Freddie Mac with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following information and documentation that Seller/Servicer identifies as confidential
elements.label: text

elements.text: (collectively, “Seller/Servicer confidential information”):
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Information regarding Seller/Servicer’s financial condition
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller/Servicer’s responses to Freddie Mac’s security questionnaire
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Information about Seller/Servicer’s information security policies
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Information related to Seller/Servicer’s business continuity/disaster recovery plans; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Information in Seller/Servicer’s system and organization controls (SOC)-1 or SOC-2
elements.label: text

elements.text: report and other third-party audit reports
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer confidential information does not include information that is:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Publicly available
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Provided to Freddie Mac by a third party that is not to Freddie Mac’s knowledge itself
elements.label: text

elements.text: under an obligation to keep the information confidential; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Independently developed by Freddie Mac or another third party
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may use Seller/Servicer confidential information for the purposes for which the
elements.label: text

elements.text: information was provided to Freddie Mac and for other internal business purposes (the
elements.label: text

elements.text: “Freddie Mac Purposes”). Freddie Mac will exercise at least the same degree of care to
elements.label: text

elements.text: preserve the confidentiality of Seller/Servicer confidential information that Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: exercises to protect its own information of a similar level of sensitivity, but in no event less
elements.label: text

elements.text: than a reasonable degree of care.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may disclose Seller/Servicer confidential information as necessary or
elements.label: text

elements.text: appropriate for Freddie Mac Purposes. Freddie Mac also may disclose it to the extent ordered
elements.label: text

elements.text: by a court or agency of competent jurisdiction, as long as:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If legally permissible, Freddie Mac promptly notifies Seller/Servicer of the order and
elements.label: text

elements.text: provides it with a reasonable opportunity to respond in such manner as it deems
elements.label: text

elements.text: appropriate to limit the disclosure, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac notifies the court or agency in writing before any disclosure that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: information is Seller/Servicer confidential information. Freddie Mac also may disclose
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer confidential information to FHFA or Freddie Mac’s other regulators
elements.label: text

elements.text: without regard to the requirements of this section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1201.9: The Mortgage file, Mortgage data and related
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: records (12/17/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ownership
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Permitted use of Mortgage data
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Language
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Ownership
elements.label: text

elements.text: All of the following will be and will remain at all times the property of Freddie Mac:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All documents in the Mortgage file
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All data related to Mortgages owned or guaranteed by Freddie Mac to which the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: obtains access in connection with any agreement with Freddie Mac, including, without
elements.label: text

elements.text: limitation:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Data in the documents in the Mortgage file (collectively, Mortgage data)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ All other documents and records related to the Mortgage of whatever kind or
elements.label: text

elements.text: description (whether prepared or originated by the Servicer or others or whether
elements.label: text

elements.text: prepared or maintained or held by the Servicer or others acting for and on behalf of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer), including all current and historical computerized data files
elements.label: text

elements.text: All of these records and Mortgage data in the possession of the Servicer are retained by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer in a custodial capacity only.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Permitted use of Mortgage data
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may use these records and Mortgage data only for the following purposes:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicing Mortgages (and, in compliance with the provisions of the Guide, retaining
elements.label: text

elements.text: subservicers to service Mortgages) on behalf of, and in the interest of, Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ As background information for the Servicer’s use related to marketing or cross-selling of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer’s own primary market products and services in compliance with applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: laws, provided that such marketing and cross-selling does not involve disclosure of these
elements.label: text

elements.text: records or Mortgage data to any third parties other than vendors assisting the Servicer in
elements.label: text

elements.text: its marketing activities who are themselves bound by these requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ As necessary, to enable a vendor to provide analytic services to the Servicer with respect
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the Servicer’s Servicing portfolio, for the Servicer’s internal use only, provided the
elements.label: text

elements.text: vendor is bound by these requirements, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ As necessary, to enable the Servicer to comply with its obligations under applicable law,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including, without limitation, any disclosures required in connection with audits by
elements.label: text

elements.text: regulatory agencies with jurisdiction over the Servicer’s operations
elements.label: text

elements.text: Except as expressly authorized by Freddie Mac in writing, Servicers may not use or disclose,
elements.label: text

elements.text: or authorize or permit third parties to use or disclose, these records or Mortgage data for any
elements.label: text

elements.text: other purpose, including, without limitation, resale or licensing of Mortgage data, either
elements.label: text

elements.text: alone or with other data.
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 8101.4(c) for additional information related to Freddie Mac audits and access to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage records and Section 8101.4(d) for additional requirements related to
elements.label: text

elements.text: confidentiality.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Language
elements.label: text

elements.text: All documents in the Mortgage file related to the origination of the Mortgage must be in
elements.label: text

elements.text: English or must be translated into English by the document originator or an unaffiliated third￾party translation service. Such translations must be attached to each non-English document,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the Seller represents and warrants to Freddie Mac that the translation is complete and
elements.label: text

elements.text: accurate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1201.10: Minority-Owned, Women-Owned and
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Disabled-Owned Business Enterprises (12/17/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: It is Freddie Mac’s policy to provide, to the maximum extent possible in balance with financially
elements.label: text

elements.text: safe and sound business practices, the opportunity for Minority-Owned, Women-Owned and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Disabled-Owned Business Enterprises to compete fairly as suppliers, contractors and
elements.label: text

elements.text: subcontractors in Freddie Mac’s business activities, taking into account both price and quality.
elements.label: text

elements.text: As an aspect of this policy, Freddie Mac encourages Seller/Servicers to ensure that Minority￾Owned, Women-Owned and Disabled-Owned Business Enterprises are given the opportunity to
elements.label: text

elements.text: compete fairly in supplying services to Seller/Servicers.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1201.11: Audit confirmation requests (12/17/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Sellers or Servicers requiring confirmation from Freddie Mac with respect to the Mortgages sold
elements.label: text

elements.text: to and serviced for Freddie Mac must use a format similar to that provided in Exhibit 65, Audit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Confirmation Request. The confirmation request must be submitted to Freddie Mac via e-mail at
elements.label: text

elements.text: Audit_Confirmation@FreddieMac.com. An e-mail response will be provided to the auditor
elements.label: text

elements.text: within five Business Days.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Below is an example of the e-mail response for audit confirmation that will be sent to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: auditor(s):
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 1301: General Responsibilities of the
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1301.1: Submission of data to Freddie Mac (12/17/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer warrants that all data and/or other information submitted to Freddie Mac is
elements.label: text

elements.text: true, complete and accurate. The Seller/Servicer agrees to complete all Freddie Mac forms
elements.label: text

elements.text: according to the instructions or guidance provided by Freddie Mac. With respect to data and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: other information transmitted to Freddie Mac through a permitted electronic medium, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer warrants that the:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Transmission complies with the requirements of the applicable Purchase Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Transmission contains all the required information
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Terms, conditions and requirements stated in the Purchase Documents have been fully
elements.label: text

elements.text: satisfied and adhered to
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1301.2: Compliance with applicable law (06/10/26)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller/Servicer obligations
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) screening; notice of OFAC match
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Anti-money laundering (AML) compliance; reporting AML non-compliance and Suspicious
elements.label: text

elements.text: Activity
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ FHFA Suspended Counterparty Program
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller lending practices
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer-maintained fair lending data elements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer responsibility for incident response program and notice of an incident
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer diversity practices – minority, women and disabled inclusion
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer adverse action notices
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Repurchase requests related to compliance with laws representations and warranties
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Address confidentiality programs (ACPs)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Seller/Servicer obligations
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer agrees to comply with all applicable federal, State and local laws,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ordinances, regulations, orders and regulator guidance (collectively, “Applicable Laws”),
elements.label: text

elements.text: including, without limitation and as amended, the following Applicable Laws:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Title VI of the Civil Rights Act of 1964
