elements.text: (b) OFAC screening; notice of OFAC match
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must comply with OFAC Regulations and must maintain a compliance
elements.label: text

elements.text: program that establishes policies and procedures for complying with OFAC Regulations. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: OFAC compliance program must include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Clear policies and procedures
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Periodic and on-going screening against the list of Specially Designated Nationals and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Blocked Persons (“OFAC SDN List”)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Process for investigating and escalating potential matches, blocking and rejecting
elements.label: text

elements.text: transactions and managing blocked accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Record retention requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ System of internal controls
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Independent testing
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A documented training program with training delivered to appropriate employees at the
elements.label: text

elements.text: time of hire and at least annually thereafter
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sellers must screen each Borrower and Guarantor against OFAC’s most recent OFAC SDN
elements.label: text

elements.text: List prior to delivery to Freddie Mac. If a Borrower or Guarantor is on the OFAC SDN List,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgage is ineligible for sale to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must periodically screen the Mortgages that it services for Freddie Mac against
elements.label: text

elements.text: the OFAC SDN List. Such screening must occur on a periodic and ongoing basis and, when
elements.label: text

elements.text: there are updates to the OFAC SDN List, be based on the Servicer’s OFAC compliance
elements.label: text

elements.text: program and be commensurate with the Servicer’s OFAC risk profile (i.e., Servicers must
elements.label: text

elements.text: screen Freddie Mac Mortgages with at least the same frequency as they do their own
elements.label: text

elements.text: portfolio). If a valid match to the OFAC SDN List is identified, the Servicer must notify
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac via e-mail within 24 hours of identifying to discuss potential changes to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing of the associated Mortgage, which may include, but are not limited to, the blocking
elements.label: text

elements.text: and/or segregation of Mortgage-related funds and ceasing certain Servicing-related activities
elements.label: text

elements.text: (see Directory 1).
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Servicer identifies or suspects a violation of OFAC sanctions involving a Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage even though the Borrower or Guarantor is not a match to the OFAC SDN List, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must notify Freddie Mac via e-mail within 24 hours of identifying the violation or
elements.label: text

elements.text: suspected violation to discuss potential changes to the Servicing of the associated Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: OFAC sanctions violations would include, but are not limited to, violations of OFAC
elements.label: text

elements.text: geographic sanctions (e.g., any indication of a prohibited provision of funds, goods or
elements.label: text

elements.text: services to a sanctioned country, individual or entity in connection with a Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage without proper authorization or licenses).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Such e-mail notification must include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac loan number
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower name
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Name, title, e-mail address and telephone number for the point of contact at the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: who will be able to discuss the OFAC SDN List match, OFAC violation or suspected
elements.label: text

elements.text: OFAC violation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon receipt of the notice, a representative from Freddie Mac will contact the Servicer to
elements.label: text

elements.text: discuss the OFAC SDN List match, OFAC violation or suspected OFAC violation and any
elements.label: text

elements.text: next steps. Freddie Mac may require the Servicer to provide additional documentation or
elements.label: text

elements.text: information regarding the matter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Notifying Freddie Mac of a confirmed match against the OFAC SDN List, OFAC violation
elements.label: text

elements.text: or suspected OFAC violation does not relieve the Servicer of any of its responsibilities under
elements.label: text

elements.text: OFAC Regulations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) AML compliance; reporting AML non-compliance and Suspicious Activity
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers subject to the AML provisions of the Bank Secrecy Act must establish and
elements.label: text

elements.text: maintain a compliance program that ensures compliance with all applicable provisions of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Bank Secrecy Act and implementing federal regulations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must, as permitted by law, notify Freddie Mac (see Directory 1) within
elements.label: text

elements.text: seven Business Days of confirmation of any instances of the Seller/Servicer’s own non￾compliance or compliance failure related to the AML requirements of the Bank Secrecy Act,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Money Laundering Control Act, Title III of the USA Patriot Act, the Anti-Money
elements.label: text

elements.text: Laundering Act of 2020 and applicable implementing federal regulations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers, including those not subject to the AML provisions of the Bank Secrecy Act,
elements.label: text

elements.text: must establish and maintain a program designed to detect and report Suspicious Activity in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the origination, delivery or Servicing of Mortgages delivered to Freddie Mac. The program
elements.label: text

elements.text: must include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A designated employee responsible for overseeing the program
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Policies, procedures and internal controls designed to detect Suspicious Activity and
elements.label: text

elements.text: report to Freddie Mac (see Directory 1) in accordance with Section 3201.2
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A documented training program with training delivered to appropriate employees at the
elements.label: text

elements.text: time of hire and at least annually thereafter
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Independent testing to monitor and maintain an adequate program
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) FHFA Suspended Counterparty Program
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must comply with the requirements set forth in Section 3101.2 applicable to
elements.label: text

elements.text: FHFA’s Suspended Counterparty Program.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Seller lending practices
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must employ business practices that promote fair lending.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Servicer-maintained fair lending data elements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must maintain the following fair lending data elements for all Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages originated on or after March 1, 2023, provided they were obtained during the
elements.label: text

elements.text: origination process:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Race of Borrower(s)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Age of Borrower(s)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ethnicity of Borrowers(s)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Gender of Borrower(s)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Preferred language of Borrower(s)
elements.label: text

elements.text: These data elements must be stored in a format that can be searched, queried and transferred.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For each Transfer of Servicing on or after March 1, 2023, if the fair lending data was
elements.label: text

elements.text: obtained during the origination process and is available, the Transferor Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: transmit the fair lending data listed above to the Transferee Servicer. Upon Transfer of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing, the Transferee Servicer must begin to maintain this data in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: above requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: As of the Effective Date of Transfer, the Transferee Servicer must begin to maintain this data
elements.label: text

elements.text: in accordance with the above requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: In the event of a future Transfer of Ownership or assumption of the Mortgage,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers may, but are not required to, update these data elements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Servicer responsibility for incident response program and notice of an incident
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must develop and maintain a “Response Program” consistent with the requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Interagency Guidance on Response Programs for Unauthorized Access to Customer
elements.label: text

elements.text: Information and Customer Notice (see 12 CFR Parts 208 and 225) and Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirement for an incident response plan pursuant to Section 1302.2(b)(xv). The Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligation to maintain a response program and provide notice of an Incident pursuant to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 1302.5 must survive the termination of the Seller/Servicer’s right to sell or service
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages pursuant to the Guide and must continue for as long as the Servicer has
elements.label: text

elements.text: possession of Mortgage files or Freddie Mac information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer knows or believes or has reasonable information from which to know or
elements.label: text

elements.text: believe, or a cybersecurity professional could reasonably conclude from the circumstances
elements.label: text

elements.text: and available information, that there may have been an Incident (as defined in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1302.5(a)) that compromises the security, confidentiality, availability, integrity or privacy of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac confidential information or Protected Information maintained by the Servicer or
elements.label: text

elements.text: in its possession or control or any other information about one or more Borrowers or other
elements.label: text

elements.text: individuals that is the subject of any state breach notification law, and if that Incident is
elements.label: text

elements.text: related to Mortgages owned or guaranteed by Freddie Mac, the Servicer must comply with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the notification requirements under Section 1302.5.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer determines not to send notices to any Borrowers and/or other individuals
elements.label: text

elements.text: affected by a certain Incident, the Servicer must provide written notice to Freddie Mac that
elements.label: text

elements.text: includes a rationale and explanation for not sending notice to all affected Borrowers and
elements.label: text

elements.text: other individuals as soon as practicable after the Incident. Notwithstanding the Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: determination not to provide such notices, Freddie Mac may direct the Servicer to provide
elements.label: text

elements.text: notices to the affected Borrowers at the Servicer’s expense.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Following the termination of the Seller/Servicer’s right to sell or service Mortgages, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer continues to be obligated to notify Freddie Mac of an Incident during such time as
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer retains the Mortgage files or Freddie Mac information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer agrees to indemnify and hold Freddie Mac harmless from and against all
elements.label: text

elements.text: claims, judgments, losses, costs and expenses incurred by Freddie Mac arising out of any
elements.label: text

elements.text: Incident.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer intends to refer to Freddie Mac in any notices it sends to Borrowers and others
elements.label: text

elements.text: on Mortgages whose information was affected by the Incident, the Servicer must notify
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac within a reasonable time prior to sending those notices. Freddie Mac must have
elements.label: text

elements.text: the opportunity to review the content of the notices prior to their delivery.
elements.label: text

elements.text: As further described in Section 1302.5, in connection with all Incidents, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: cooperate with Freddie Mac, including, without limitation, by providing all requested
elements.label: text

elements.text: information and assistance, such as:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Enabling Freddie Mac to comply with its legal obligations
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Providing technical or forensics information to determine the extent of the Incident
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Supplying other information and assistance to identify, evaluate and manage any issues
elements.label: text

elements.text: arising out of the Incident as Freddie Mac deems necessary or advisable
elements.label: text

elements.text: (h) Servicer diversity practices – minority, women and disabled inclusion
elements.label: text

elements.text: In accordance with regulations adopted by FHFA, 12 C.F.R. Pt. 1207, pursuant to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Housing and Economic Recovery Act of 2008, 12 U.S.C. § 4520 (“HERA”), each
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer will:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Practice the principles of equal employment opportunity and non-discrimination in all its
elements.label: text

elements.text: business activities
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Contractually require each subcontractor that the Servicer engages to provide services or
elements.label: text

elements.text: goods to Freddie Mac to practice the principles of equal employment opportunity and
elements.label: text

elements.text: non-discrimination in all its business activities, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Upon request, provide Freddie Mac with information and appropriate certifications
elements.label: text

elements.text: regarding:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The diversity status of the Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The diversity status of subcontractors the Seller/Servicer engages to originate
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage under the Purchase Documents and/or provide services or goods to Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac with respect to Servicing under the Purchase Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The amounts Freddie Mac pays to the Servicer for Servicing under the Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The amounts the Servicer pays to subcontractors to provide services or goods to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac with respect to Servicing under the Purchase Documents, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any other information Freddie Mac requests to comply with HERA and applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: diversity and inclusion regulations
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Servicer adverse action notices
elements.label: text

elements.text: Below are Servicer responsibilities when Freddie Mac participates in evaluating a Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: for a workout or relief option that results in a denial:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If Freddie Mac participated in evaluating a Borrower for a workout or relief option that is
elements.label: text

elements.text: not approved, Freddie Mac will provide the reasons for denial
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If Freddie Mac participated in the decision and if the decision gives rise to an obligation
elements.label: text

elements.text: to provide the Borrower with a notice or notices under applicable law, including, but not
elements.label: text

elements.text: limited to, adverse action notices required by the Equal Credit Opportunity Act or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Fair Credit Reporting Act, then the Servicer must provide such notices to the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: on behalf of Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ In these cases, the notices provided by the Servicer to the Borrower must identify both
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer and Freddie Mac as having participated in the evaluation of the workout or
elements.label: text

elements.text: relief option and the decision to deny the request.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (j) Repurchase requests related to compliance with laws representations and warranties
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will only issue a repurchase request for Seller violations of law that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Could be expected to impair Freddie Mac’s or its Servicer’s ability to enforce the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note or Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Impose assignee liability, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Are found to have been violations of, or if Freddie Mac has made a finding based on
elements.label: text

elements.text: the facts available to Freddie Mac that a violation may have occurred of, one or more
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the following laws or related regulations:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ OFAC Regulations
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Fair Housing Act
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Anti-discrimination provisions of the Equal Credit Opportunity Act (ECOA)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Securities Exchange Act of 1934, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Unfair, deceptive, or abusive acts or practices under federal and State laws
elements.label: text

elements.text: (UDAAP)
elements.label: text

elements.text: With respect to UDAAP, Freddie Mac will take into consideration published federal and
elements.label: text

elements.text: State announcements of interpretations as well as all published judicial and
elements.label: text

elements.text: administrative decisions and will not enforce a repurchase if the matter can be cured by
elements.label: text

elements.text: remediation to the injured party and the Seller makes such remediation. However, three
elements.label: text

elements.text: or more years after the Settlement Date of a Mortgage, Freddie Mac may not seek
elements.label: text

elements.text: repurchase on UDAAP grounds regarding a specific practice unless:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A lender self reports
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A federal or State enforcement authority has indicated, asserted or claimed that such
elements.label: text

elements.text: practice violates or may violate UDAAP
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A federal or State court has held that a specific practice violates UDAAP
elements.label: text

elements.text: A repurchase demand based on a compliance with law violation by the Seller will include
elements.label: text

elements.text: supporting facts and findings made by Freddie Mac in the course of considering the facts and
elements.label: text

elements.text: circumstances before it. Freddie Mac’s determination that a violation has occurred must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: consistent with the facts and circumstances provided by the Seller and any other information
elements.label: text

elements.text: obtained by Freddie Mac as part of its evaluation of the situation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When Freddie Mac issues a repurchase request to the Seller in connection with a failure to
elements.label: text

elements.text: comply with laws and there is pending litigation underway involving that same issue or a
elements.label: text

elements.text: government agency with authority to make a determination regarding the issue has publicly
elements.label: text

elements.text: stated that it is reviewing the issue, the Seller will not be required to repurchase until 30 days
elements.label: text

elements.text: after the litigation has been dismissed, settled or concluded at trial in an adjudication or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: governmental agency has made a final determination, as applicable (collectively, “the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Resolution”). After the Resolution, the Seller may request that Freddie Mac review the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appropriateness of the repurchase request in light of the Resolution. Freddie Mac will
elements.label: text

elements.text: withdraw the repurchase request where appropriate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will not issue a repurchase request to the Seller based on violations of the ability
elements.label: text

elements.text: to repay provisions under the Truth in Lending Act (“ATR”) unless a court or regulator
elements.label: text

elements.text: concludes the Mortgage did not comply with ATR.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section does not limit Freddie Mac’s rights to issue repurchase requests for Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: breach of its obligations regarding compliance with Applicable Laws.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (k) ACPs
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Borrower may be a participant in a State-sponsored ACP that provides the Borrower with a
elements.label: text

elements.text: substitute mailing address to be used instead of the Borrower’s home address for all mail
elements.label: text

elements.text: correspondence. Sellers must comply with the applicable federal and State laws related to
elements.label: text

elements.text: ACPs.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Within five Business Days after the Funding Date of any Mortgage for which the Borrower is
elements.label: text

elements.text: a participant in an ACP, the Seller must provide the following information to Freddie Mac at
elements.label: text

elements.text: the e-mail address Loan_Delivery_Funding_Ops@FreddieMac.com:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac loan number
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower name
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Borrower ACP mailing address (including, when applicable, any lot number or required
elements.label: text

elements.text: uniquely identifiable number)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will use the provided substitute ACP mailing address when required by law for
elements.label: text

elements.text: mailing correspondence to the Borrower, such as the Mortgage transfer disclosures required
elements.label: text

elements.text: under the Truth in Lending Act.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1301.3: Inspection of records (12/17/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer agrees to allow Freddie Mac, during normal business hours, to inspect all of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller/Servicer’s books and records pertaining to its Mortgage operations and to any
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages purchased by Freddie Mac, including, without limitation, any Seller/Servicer:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Organizational and associated documents such as its articles of organization, operating
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreement, employment agreements and management agreements, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Contract(s) with a Related Third Party
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may request an inspection of records, either before or after termination of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Contract and related Servicing Contract Rights, whether in whole or in part, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must permit Freddie Mac at any time during normal business hours to inspect the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage files and all of the Servicer’s records pertaining to Mortgage operations related to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The inspection may include a review of the Servicer’s information security and business
elements.label: text

elements.text: continuation programs, copies of independent audits and assessments addressing privacy and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: data security (e.g., system and organization control reports).
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgage files or Freddie Mac information, Freddie Mac may alternatively request a copy of
elements.label: text

elements.text: .the files
elements.label: text

elements.text: These inspection rights will survive for the period during which the Servicer retains Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: files or Freddie Mac information, including pursuant to Applicable Laws. Additionally, for as
elements.label: text

elements.text: long as the Servicer retains possession of Mortgage files or Freddie Mac information, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Securely store and retain such information in accordance with the standards set forth in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sections 1302.1 and 1302.2; and

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer should carefully read the Purchase Documents. The Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: acknowledges that Freddie Mac and any transferees or assignees of Freddie Mac purchase
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer’s warranties and representations and on its compliance with the agreements,
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements, terms and conditions set forth in the Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Review of Mortgages by Freddie Mac prior to purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Before the Freddie Mac Funding Date (Cash programs) or Settlement Date of the UMBS®,
elements.label: text

elements.text: MBS or WAC ARM PC (Guarantor programs), Freddie Mac may, in its discretion, refuse to
elements.label: text

elements.text: purchase any Mortgage if it determines any of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage is not of investment quality
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller has failed to satisfy or has breached any of the provisions of the Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents

elements.text: ■ Any of the warranties or representations of the Seller to Freddie Mac are untrue
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller has failed to provide Freddie Mac with information that is true, complete and
elements.label: text

elements.text: accurate as to the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: For each Mortgage it refuses to purchase, Freddie Mac may require the Seller to substitute in
elements.label: text

elements.text: its place another Mortgage that is satisfactory to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Warranties and representations by the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: As of the Delivery Date, the Funding Date or the Settlement Date (as applicable), and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: date of any substitution of Mortgages pursuant to the Purchase Documents, the Seller makes
elements.label: text

elements.text: the warranties and representations in this section for each Mortgage purchased by Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac. The Seller is fully liable for all warranties and representations made to Freddie Mac,
elements.label: text

elements.text: regardless of whether the Seller originated the Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) General warranties and representations
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The terms, conditions and requirements stated in the Purchase Documents have been
elements.label: text

elements.text: fully satisfied
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. All warranties and representations of the Seller are true and correct
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The Seller is in compliance with its agreements contained in the Purchase Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. The Seller has not misstated, misrepresented or omitted any material fact about the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: and notices required under Applicable Laws and the terms of the Note and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Security Instrument

elements.label: text

elements.text: interest rate or the monthly payment correctly and given on a timely basis any and all
elements.label: text

elements.text: notices of such adjustments completely in accordance with the terms of the Note and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Security Instrument and with the requirements of applicable law

elements.label: text

elements.text: each conventional Mortgage delivered with an appraisal report, the appraisal report
elements.label: text

elements.text: was obtained in a manner consistent with Exhibit 35
elements.label: text

elements.text: a role, as described in Section 3101.1, in the origination or sale of such Mortgage or
elements.label: text

elements.text: the related real estate transaction
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9. Solely with respect to a Mortgage registered with MERS®, MERS is the mortgagee of
elements.label: text

elements.text: record (either by being named in the Security Instrument as nominee for the Seller or
elements.label: text

elements.text: as the assignee in a recorded assignment of the Security Instrument), or, where
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable, MERS is not the mortgagee of record for purposes of MERS iRegistration
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: Requirements, and, for each conventional Mortgage delivered with a PDR, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property data collection was obtained in a manner consistent with Exhibit 42
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon Freddie Mac’s written request, either before or after the Funding Date, the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: will supply evidence satisfactory to Freddie Mac of the Seller’s compliance with any
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Documents.

elements.label: text

elements.text: confirm to Freddie Mac that the Mortgages purchased by Freddie Mac are free and
elements.label: text

elements.text: clear of any and all security interests as of the Funding Date or the Settlement Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller warrants to Freddie Mac that as of the Freddie Mac Funding Date (Cash
elements.label: text

elements.text: programs) or Settlement Date of the UMBS, MBS or WAC ARM PC (Guarantor
elements.label: text

elements.text: programs), the Mortgage is not subject to any other interest.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller further warrants and agrees not to sell, assign, convey, hypothecate, pledge
elements.label: text

elements.text: or in any other way transfer (conditionally or otherwise) its interest in a Mortgage that
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac has purchased except as expressly permitted in the Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: The warranties and representations in the Purchase Documents for any Mortgage purchased
elements.label: text

elements.text: by Freddie Mac survive payment of the purchase price by Freddie Mac. The warranties and
elements.label: text

elements.text: representations are not affected by any investigation made by, or on behalf of, Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: except when expressly waived in writing by Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When any party has purchased a Mortgage from Freddie Mac that Freddie Mac previously
elements.label: text

elements.text: purchased from a Seller, Freddie Mac may exercise any rights or remedies at law or in equity
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 2301 or Section 9102.1.

elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac may, at any time and without limitation, require the Seller or the Servicer, at the Seller’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: or the Servicer’s expense, to make such endorsements to and assignments and recordations of
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Eligible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will not exercise its remedies in connection with breaches of representations
elements.label: text

elements.text: and warranties described in Section 1301.6(a) for Mortgages that meet the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage must have a Freddie Mac Settlement Date on or after July 1, 2014
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage must be a conventional Mortgage delivered to Freddie Mac through a flow
elements.label: text

elements.text: purchase contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage must not be a Mortgage that Freddie Mac and the Seller/Servicer have
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreed is subject to any credit enhancement other than primary mortgage insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage must have either an acceptable payment history or a satisfactory
elements.label: text

elements.text: conclusion of a Freddie Mac quality control review as more fully described below:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Acceptable payment history
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgage has an acceptable payment history if it meets the following eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ For Mortgages other than Freddie Mac Relief RefinanceSM Mortgages and Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac Enhanced Relief Refinance® Mortgages:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Following the Freddie Mac Settlement Date, the Borrower (i) made the first 36
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly payments due with no more than two 30-day Delinquencies and no 60-
elements.label: text

elements.text: day or greater Delinquencies; and (ii) must not be 30 or more days delinquent
elements.label: text

elements.text: with respect to the 36th monthly payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ For Freddie Mac Relief Refinance Mortgages and Freddie Mac Enhanced Relief
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refinance Mortgages:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Following the Freddie Mac Settlement Date, the Borrower made the first 12
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly payments due with no 30-day or greater Delinquencies, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Following the Freddie Mac Settlement Date, the Borrower (i) made the first 36
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly payments due with no more than two 30-day Delinquencies, and no 60-
elements.label: text

elements.text: day or greater Delinquencies; and (ii) must not be 30 or more days delinquent
elements.label: text

elements.text: with respect to the 36th monthly payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ In addition, during the applicable payment history period:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ With the exception of temporary subsidy buydown arrangements permitted by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Documents, neither the Seller/Servicer nor any third party may escrow
elements.label: text

elements.text: or advance funds to be used for payment of any monthly installment, principal,
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest or other charge payable under the terms of the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage must not have been subject to a repayment plan, or otherwise have
elements.label: text

elements.text: been modified from its original terms, except as otherwise stated below for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: disaster-related forbearance plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage must not have an outstanding request for a repurchase, a
elements.label: text

elements.text: repurchase alternative or a make-whole
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Mortgage may have been subject to a disaster-related forbearance plan during the
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable payment history period as a result of the Borrower being impacted by an
elements.label: text

elements.text: Eligible Disaster pursuant to Chapter 8404, provided that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage is fully reinstated, as described in Section 9203.1(c), or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower agrees to a repayment plan and complies with the terms of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: repayment plan, restoring the Mortgage to a current status, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The disaster-related forbearance transitions into a Disaster Payment Deferral,
elements.label: text

elements.text: restoring the Mortgage to a current status, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The disaster-related forbearance transitions into a permanent modification,
elements.label: text

elements.text: restoring the Mortgage to a current status, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For all four options described above, payments due during the disaster-related
elements.label: text

elements.text: forbearance period are considered to have been made on time for the purposes of
elements.label: text

elements.text: this section
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: The Servicer must report the Borrower’s payment status of the Mortgage in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with Section 8303.1(c).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Satisfactory conclusion of a Freddie Mac quality control review
elements.label: text

elements.text: For a Mortgage to obtain a satisfactory conclusion of a Freddie Mac quality control
elements.label: text

elements.text: review, it must meet one of the following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac completes a quality control review of the Mortgage file, which includes
elements.label: text

elements.text: a review of the credit underwriting and eligibility of the Borrower, the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises (including its value) and the project in which the Mortgaged Premises is
elements.label: text

elements.text: located, if applicable (“quality control review”), and determines that the Mortgage is
elements.label: text

elements.text: acceptable (that is, the Mortgage is not subject to a repurchase request)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac completes the quality control review and determines the Mortgage is not
elements.label: text

elements.text: acceptable because of a loan deficiency that is curable, and the Seller/Servicer cures
elements.label: text

elements.text: the deficiency to Freddie Mac’s satisfaction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example: If the Mortgage file delivered to Freddie Mac did not contain the required
elements.label: text

elements.text: verification of income, the loan deficiency would be deemed to be corrected if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer provided the missing documentation within the time frame specified.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Another example of an action taken to correct a loan deficiency is rectifying a prior
elements.label: text

elements.text: lien by producing evidence of a recorded satisfaction or release of such prior lien
elements.label: text

elements.text: within the time frame specified.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac completes the quality control review and determines the Mortgage is not
elements.label: text

elements.text: acceptable, but Freddie Mac and the Seller/Servicer agreed upon an alternative to
elements.label: text

elements.text: repurchase that has since expired or terminated by its terms.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example: If Freddie Mac determined a Mortgage was not acceptable and, as a
elements.label: text

elements.text: repurchase alternative, Freddie Mac and the Seller/Servicer agreed that the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: would be subject to recourse for five years, then the Seller/Servicer will be relieved
elements.label: text

elements.text: from Freddie Mac’s enforcement of the representations and warranties described in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 1301.6(a) at the end of the five-year period. Other possible repurchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: alternatives include indemnification, make-whole arrangements and certain split loss
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Life-of-loan representations and warranties
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages that meet the eligibility requirements set forth in (b) above, the Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: will not be relieved from Freddie Mac’s enforcement of its representations and warranties
elements.label: text

elements.text: with respect to the following matters, even if such issues are referenced in the Guide topics,
elements.label: text

elements.text: chapters or sections in the table above. Instead, the Seller/Servicer will be responsible for all
elements.label: text

elements.text: such representations and warranties for the life of the loan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Charter matters
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each Mortgage must be eligible for purchase under the Freddie Mac Charter Act in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following respects:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Mortgage must be secured by a residential property that is located within any of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the 50 States, the District of Columbia, Guam, Puerto Rico or the U.S. Virgin Islands
elements.label: text

elements.text: at the time of Freddie Mac’s purchase, as set forth in Section 4201.1 and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: definition of a State in the Glossary
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The original UPB of the Mortgage must not exceed the maximum original loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: amounts set forth in Sections 4203.1(c) and 4603.2
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Mortgage must not be secured by vacant land or property primarily used for
elements.label: text

elements.text: agriculture, farming or commercial enterprise at the time of Freddie Mac’s purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Mortgage must be secured by a residential property consisting of one to four
elements.label: text

elements.text: dwelling units at the time of Freddie Mac’s purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Any Mortgage with an LTV ratio in excess of 80% at the time of Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: purchase must (i) have mortgage insurance on the portion of the Mortgage in excess
elements.label: text

elements.text: of 80% of the property’s value (determined in accordance with Section 4701.1), or in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the case of Freddie Mac Relief Refinance Mortgages and Freddie Mac Enhanced
elements.label: text

elements.text: Relief Refinance Mortgages, meet the applicable mortgage insurance requirements in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Guide, (ii) be sold with recourse, within the meaning of Section 6201.2(b)(i)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Misstatements, misrepresentations and omissions
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgage must not have any misstatements misrepresentations or omissions
elements.label: text

elements.text: (“misrepresentations”) by any party to the Mortgage transaction (including, but not
elements.label: text

elements.text: limited to, the Seller, Borrowers, property sellers, builders, real estate agents, lenders,
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgage brokers, loan officers, originators, appraisers, appraisal companies, closing
elements.label: text

elements.text: agents, title companies or other third party vendors) pertaining to the requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: described in the topics, chapters and sections of the Guide set forth in the table above that
elements.label: text

elements.text: are made with or without the Seller’s knowledge and that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Involve three or more Mortgages sold to Freddie Mac by the same Seller, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Were made pursuant to a common pattern of activity in connection with the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: origination or sale based on information in the Mortgage file or other facts or
elements.label: text

elements.text: circumstances that existed on the Settlement Date that involved at least one party
elements.label: text

elements.text: common to all the Mortgages (if the common party is the Seller, then the same
elements.label: text

elements.text: individual; if the common party is a third party, then the same individual or entity),
elements.label: text

elements.text: and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Are “significant” in that, using true and accurate information, either Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: determines that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The Mortgage would not have been eligible for sale under the terms of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller’s Purchase Documents in effect on the Settlement Date, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The Mortgage would have been eligible for sale, but under different terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: For purposes of finding three or more Mortgages to constitute the pattern, both
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages that have obtained relief under the framework and Mortgages that have not
elements.label: text

elements.text: obtained relief may be counted. If Mortgages that have not obtained relief are counted
elements.label: text

elements.text: for purposes of establishing a pattern, those Mortgages must meet all requirements of
elements.label: text

elements.text: this exclusion (i.e., three or more Mortgages, common pattern of activity,
elements.label: text

elements.text: significance) in order for Freddie Mac to enforce a remedy for each Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In determining whether a misrepresentation is significant, Freddie Mac will rely on its
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Product Advisor simulator, which approximates the Risk Class at the time of
elements.label: text

elements.text: delivery. Freddie Mac will compare the Loan Product Advisor simulator assessment
elements.label: text

elements.text: using true and accurate information with the Loan Product Advisor simulator
elements.label: text

elements.text: assessment received at the time of delivery.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A misrepresentation will be considered significant only if the Mortgage receives a
elements.label: text

elements.text: worse Loan Product Advisor assessment from the simulator than was received at the
elements.label: text

elements.text: time of delivery to Freddie Mac, except that Freddie Mac will also take into account
elements.label: text

elements.text: any applicable negotiated terms of business and the impact of any undisclosed
elements.label: text

elements.text: concessions, concealed transaction terms or other violations of the Seller’s Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents that are involved in the misrepresentation but are not assessed by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Product Advisor simulator when determining significance. Freddie Mac will
elements.label: text

elements.text: notify the Seller/Servicer of any such undisclosed matters or violations that are
elements.label: text

elements.text: considered in connection with determining significance and will provide the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer with documentation supporting the significance determination.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac determines that the Mortgage would have been eligible for sale but
elements.label: text

elements.text: under different terms than those under which the Mortgage was sold, as described in
elements.label: text

elements.text: number 2 of the third bullet above, Freddie Mac will not seek repurchase but instead
elements.label: text

elements.text: will re-price the Mortgage, consistent with the Seller’s Purchase Documents in effect
elements.label: text

elements.text: on the Settlement Date, to reflect the true risk profile of the Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: As an exception to the above, Mortgages involving fraud will be subject to
elements.label: text

elements.text: repurchase, regardless of whether the above test (three or more Mortgages, common
elements.label: text

elements.text: pattern of activity, significance) has been met. For purposes of this life-of-loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: representation and warranty only, “fraud” is established either by:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An adjudicated claim affirming fraud by or against the Seller or other party to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage transaction, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac finding clear and convincing evidence that a Seller or other party to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgage transaction knowingly executed or participated in a scheme or
elements.label: text

elements.text: artifice in connection with the underwriting, origination or sale of a Mortgage to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Defraud Freddie Mac or any other party to the Mortgage transaction, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Obtain any moneys, funds, credits, assets, securities or other properties from
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac or any other party to the Mortgage transaction by means of
elements.label: text

elements.text: fraudulent pretenses, representations or promises
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Data inaccuracies
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgage must not have any Uniform Loan Delivery Dataset (ULDD) data
elements.label: text

elements.text: inaccuracies pertaining to the requirements described in the topics, chapters and sections
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Guide set forth in the table above, if and to the extent:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The data inaccuracies affect five or more Mortgages and involve the same delivery
elements.label: text

elements.text: data element(s), and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The ULDD data differs from the information in the Mortgage file, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The data inaccuracies are “significant” in that, using the information in the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: file to qualify the Borrower, Mortgaged Premises and/or project in which the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises is located, Freddie Mac determines that either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The Mortgage would not have been eligible for sale under the terms of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller’s Purchase Documents in effect on the Settlement Date, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The Mortgage would have been eligible for sale but under different terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: For purposes of finding five or more Mortgages involving the same delivery data
elements.label: text

elements.text: element inaccuracy, both Mortgages that have obtained relief under the framework
elements.label: text

elements.text: and Mortgages that have not obtained relief may be counted. If Mortgages that have
elements.label: text

elements.text: not obtained relief are counted for purposes of establishing that there were five or
elements.label: text

elements.text: more Mortgages with the same delivery data element inaccuracy, those Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: must meet all other requirements of this exclusion (i.e., same delivery data elements,
elements.label: text

elements.text: data differs from information in the Mortgage file, significance) in order for Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac to enforce a remedy for each Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In determining whether the data inaccuracy is significant, Freddie Mac will rely on its
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Product Advisor simulator, which approximates the Risk Class at the time of
elements.label: text

elements.text: delivery. Freddie Mac will compare the Loan Product Advisor simulator assessment
elements.label: text

elements.text: using true and accurate information with the Loan Product Advisor simulator
elements.label: text

elements.text: assessment received at the time of delivery.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A data inaccuracy will be considered significant only if the Mortgage receives a
elements.label: text

elements.text: worse Loan Product Advisor assessment from the simulator than was received at the
elements.label: text

elements.text: time of delivery to Freddie Mac, except that Freddie Mac will also take into account
elements.label: text

elements.text: any applicable negotiated terms of business when determining significance. Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac will provide the Seller/Servicer with documentation supporting the significance
elements.label: text

elements.text: determination.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac determines that the Mortgage would have been eligible for sale but
elements.label: text

elements.text: under different terms than those under which the Mortgage was sold, as described in
elements.label: text

elements.text: number 2 of the third bullet above, Freddie Mac will not seek repurchase but instead
elements.label: text

elements.text: will re-price the Mortgage, consistent with the Seller’s Purchase Documents in effect
elements.label: text

elements.text: on the Settlement Date, to reflect the true risk profile of the Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Clear title/First Lien priority: The Mortgage must be enforceable as a First Lien
elements.label: text

elements.text: (with no pending condemnation proceedings) and have clear title through
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Compliance with laws: The Mortgage must comply with all applicable federal,
elements.label: text

elements.text: State and local laws, ordinances, regulations and orders, including, without
elements.label: text

elements.text: limitation, State anti-predatory lending laws and regulations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For all Mortgages with Application Received Dates on or after July 1, 2021, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: all Mortgages with Settlement Dates after August 31, 2021, in addition to
elements.label: text

elements.text: ensuring compliance with applicable laws, Sellers must ensure that all ATR
elements.label: text

elements.text: Covered Mortgages satisfy the QM requirements of the Revised General QM
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rule, even if the Seller is not required by law or regulation to comply with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Revised General QM Rule.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Unacceptable Mortgage products: The Mortgage must be a Mortgage product
elements.label: text

elements.text: acceptable for sale to Freddie Mac. The Seller/Servicer will not be relieved of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s enforcement of its representations and warranties for unacceptable
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage products, including, but not limited to, the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A Mortgage with an interest-only feature
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A graduated-payment Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A Mortgage originated with stated or no income and/or asset documentation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (Freddie Mac Relief Refinance Mortgages and Freddie Mac Enhanced Relief
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refinance Mortgages are not considered a Mortgage originated with stated or
elements.label: text

elements.text: no income and/or asset documentation.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A Mortgage subject to negative amortization
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A construction loan (other than a Construction to Permanent Mortgage)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A daily simple interest Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A Prepayment Penalty Mortgage with an Application Received Date on or
elements.label: text

elements.text: after January 10, 2014 or a Freddie Mac Settlement Date after July 31, 2014
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A reverse Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A Mortgage with balloon payments (with or without a reset option)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A second Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1301.7: Non-discrimination (12/17/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Freddie Mac expects all Seller/Servicers with whom Freddie Mac does business to practice the
elements.label: text

elements.text: principles of equal opportunity and non-discrimination in all business activities. As such,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, age, marital
elements.label: text

elements.text: status, disability, veteran status, genetic information (including family medical history),
elements.label: text

elements.text: pregnancy, parental status, familial status, national origin, ethnicity, sexual orientation, gender
elements.label: text

elements.text: identity or other characteristics protected by law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 1302: Information Security and Business
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Continuity Planning
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1302.1: Overview of information security and business
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: continuity planning requirements (09/11/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Information security, business continuity and disaster recovery planning
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Minimizing Freddie Mac’s risk of loss
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Information security, business continuity and disaster recovery
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Information security, business continuity and disaster recovery planning
elements.label: text

elements.text: This chapter contains the minimum information security program requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must implement to reduce the impact and likelihood of unauthorized persons
elements.label: text

elements.text: (or authorized persons with malicious or unlawful intentions) from gaining access to Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac’s proprietary information, data and Protected Information in:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Systems, as defined in Section 2401.1(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller/Servicers’ files, records, storage facilities and systems
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Files, records, storage facilities and systems of any Related Third Party
elements.label: text

elements.text: This chapter also includes the minimum requirements for a Seller/Servicer’s business
elements.label: text

elements.text: continuity plan and disaster recovery plan to support continuation of critical business
elements.label: text

elements.text: processes necessary to comply with the Seller/Servicer’s Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Minimizing Freddie Mac’s risk of loss
elements.label: text

elements.text: To minimize Freddie Mac’s risk of loss in the event of a disaster or unexpected disruption to
elements.label: text

elements.text: critical business processes, a Seller/Servicer must have and maintain an information security
elements.label: text

elements.text: program, business continuity and disaster recovery plan(s) that ensure its ongoing ability to
elements.label: text

elements.text: conduct business operations with Freddie Mac. Information security program, business
elements.label: text

elements.text: continuity plan and disaster recovery plan requirements must extend to the confidentiality,
elements.label: text

elements.text: integrity and availability of Freddie Mac confidential information (as defined in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1201.8(a)) and Protected Information (as defined in Section 8101.4(d)) retained by a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer following Freddie Mac’s termination of the Seller/Servicer’s right to sell or
elements.label: text

elements.text: service Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Information security, business continuity and disaster recovery
elements.label: text

elements.text: The information security, business continuity and disaster recovery minimum requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (together, the “Minimum Requirements”) are not intended to replace the Seller/Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: standards, policies and procedures but are intended to require certain minimum controls that
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be in place as part of such standards, policies and procedures. If a Seller/Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: regulator has established information security and/or business continuity plan and/or disaster
elements.label: text

elements.text: recovery plan requirements that exceed Freddie Mac’s Minimum Requirements, or if a
elements.label: text

elements.text: provision of the Guide or the Seller/Servicer’s other Purchase Documents requires more
elements.label: text

elements.text: stringent minimum requirements, then the more rigorous requirements shall apply. A
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer’s compliance with the Minimum Requirements will not relieve the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer from any liability arising or accruing under any other provision in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Documents. A Seller/Servicer’s failure to comply with the Minimum Requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: may result in termination of the Seller/Servicer’s access to any or all Systems. In addition,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may take other actions available under the Guide, the Seller/Servicer’s other
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Documents, any user agreement or law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The National Institute of Standards and Technology and International Organization for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Standardization/International Electrotechnical Commission provide detailed guidance on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: components of a successful information security program, business continuity plan and
elements.label: text

elements.text: related activities. Seller/Servicers are strongly encouraged to review these standards and
elements.label: text

elements.text: guidance to ensure their practices align with industry best practices.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1302.2: Information security (05/11/26)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Defined terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Information security minimum requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Defined terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers should be familiar with the following defined terms as they relate to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of Section 1302.2:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Information security defined terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: A
elements.label: text

elements.text: Authentication The process in which a system verifies the identity and role of an
elements.label: text

elements.text: individual, usually based on some form of credential(s)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (password/ID, token, etc.).
elements.label: text

elements.text: E
elements.label: text

elements.text: Encryption The process of encoding or obfuscating messages or information in
elements.label: text

elements.text: such a way that only authorized parties can read it.
elements.label: text

elements.text: F
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Critical Data
elements.label: text

elements.text: All information necessary for the Seller/Servicer to perform its
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligations under the Seller/Servicer’s Purchase Documents,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including, but not limited to, Freddie Mac confidential information
elements.label: text

elements.text: and Protected Information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: V
elements.label: text

elements.text: Vulnerability
elements.label: text

elements.text: Management
elements.label: text

elements.text: The process of identifying and testing known software
elements.label: text

elements.text: vulnerabilities within a system and prioritizing remediation
elements.label: text

elements.text: according to each vulnerability’s likelihood of occurrence and how
elements.label: text

elements.text: the exploitation of the vulnerability would impact the system.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Information security minimum requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Information security program
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must define an individual or group of individuals responsible for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: development of information security requirements, including the adoption,
elements.label: text

elements.text: implementation, maintenance and administration of written minimum-security standards,
elements.label: text

elements.text: policies and procedures that responsibly address critical issues such as user
elements.label: text

elements.text: responsibilities (e.g., “Acceptable Use”); ownership of and access to information;
elements.label: text

elements.text: baseline security practices; physical, administrative and technical security protection
elements.label: text

elements.text: mechanisms and other requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Not less than annually, Seller/Servicers must review and assess the adequacy of their
elements.label: text

elements.text: information security policies and procedures used in connection with the selling and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing of Freddie Mac Mortgages to ensure compliance with the Guide, their other
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Documents and industry best practices (including as set forth by the National
elements.label: text

elements.text: Institute of Standards and Technology and International Organization for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Standardization/International Electrotechnical Commission standards).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon request of Freddie Mac, Seller/Servicers must make their information security
elements.label: text

elements.text: program policies and procedures available; further, upon request of Freddie Mac,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must provide an attestation executed by a duly authorized corporate
elements.label: text

elements.text: officer of the adequacy of these policies and procedures, including following the
elements.label: text

elements.text: termination of a Seller/Servicer’s right to sell or service Mortgages, or the occurrence of
elements.label: text

elements.text: an Incident (as defined in Section 1302.5).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Human resources security
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following are requirements related to human resources security:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Pre-employment screening: Seller/Servicers must conduct, or retain a qualified third
elements.label: text

elements.text: party to conduct, thorough background verification checks (screening) for all
elements.label: text

elements.text: candidates for employment or contractor status who will have access to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: confidential information, Protected Information or Systems (as defined in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Code of conduct or non-disclosure agreement: Prior to being granted access to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac confidential information, Protected Information or Systems,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must require all employees, contractors and third parties to (i) sign a
elements.label: text

elements.text: non-disclosure agreement or (ii) be subject to a code of conduct, which in either case
elements.label: text

elements.text: includes obligations to restrict the use or disclosure of and to maintain as confidential
elements.label: text

elements.text: all Freddie Mac confidential information
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Protected Information and information related to or contained in Systems: The code
elements.label: text

elements.text: of conduct must be acknowledged by the employee, contractor or third party, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: must address at least the following subjects:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Appropriate use of company assets
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Information protection, including non-disclosure and confidentiality
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Records management
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Information security and privacy
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Business courtesies
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Personal investments and insider trading
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Conflicts of interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Information security awareness, education and training: At least annually,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must provide information security awareness training to all
elements.label: text

elements.text: employees and contractors who have access to Freddie Mac confidential information,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Protected Information and/or Systems.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The training should incorporate current cybersecurity threats, including phishing,
elements.label: text

elements.text: social engineering, supply chain attacks, malware/ransomware, credential
elements.label: text

elements.text: compromise via weak password hygiene, insider threats, artificial intelligence (AI)-
elements.label: text

elements.text: powered tactics (e.g., deepfakes, targeted phishing content) and threats to AI systems
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e.g., model inversion, data poisoning, prompt injection). When necessary, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: organization personnel and partners must receive AI risk management training to
elements.label: text

elements.text: enable them to perform their duties and responsibilities consistent with related
elements.label: text

elements.text: policies, procedures and agreements. At a minimum, the training must address the
elements.label: text

elements.text: importance of data security, provide details on roles and responsibilities for all users
elements.label: text

elements.text: in protecting information at the Seller/Servicer (along with practical ways to
elements.label: text

elements.text: incorporate information security into daily routines) and include the specific
elements.label: text

elements.text: processes in place for protecting Freddie Mac confidential information and Protected
elements.label: text

elements.text: Information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Physical and environmental security controls
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must create and maintain:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A physical security control program of the organization’s buildings and facilities that
elements.label: text

elements.text: contain information systems, designed to detect, monitor and prevent unauthorized
elements.label: text

elements.text: access and to respond to physical security incidents using real-time physical intrusion
elements.label: text

elements.text: alarms and surveillance equipment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An updated list of personnel with authorized access to facilities where information
elements.label: text

elements.text: systems reside, including an access privilege review performed not less than annually
elements.label: text

elements.text: and upon the departure of any authorized personnel
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Environmental controls to monitor, mitigate and protect the organization with regards
elements.label: text

elements.text: to a loss of connectivity, access to or integrity of information and damage caused by
elements.label: text

elements.text: natural disasters or man-made incidents such as fire, earthquake, flood, hurricane,
elements.label: text

elements.text: tornado or weather-related adverse conditions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A clean desk policy that ensures that Freddie Mac confidential information and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Protected Information are stored securely
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Communications and operations management
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must implement technical security measures designed to monitor for,
elements.label: text

elements.text: mitigate against and prevent malicious software, stop unwanted spam and traffic and to
elements.label: text

elements.text: protect against unauthorized use of wireless connections. Measures must include those
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided in the remainder of this section or meet industry best practices, whichever is
elements.label: text

elements.text: more stringent.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Data transmission and data loss prevention
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maintain a data loss prevention/transmission protection mechanism and related
elements.label: text

elements.text: written policy establishing requirements to protect the confidentiality and integrity of
elements.label: text

elements.text: information exchange using technology applications or information systems
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure adequate and up-to-date data loss prevention software is used and a
elements.label: text

elements.text: corresponding management process is in place to scan for sensitive information
elements.label: text

elements.text: stored on media and outgoing transmissions over public communication paths as well
elements.label: text

elements.text: as to restrict the transfer of data to USB and other removable media devices at the
elements.label: text

elements.text: desktop level
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Not transmit to System(s) or the Uniform Collateral Data Portal®, through an
elements.label: text

elements.text: application programming interface or otherwise, any Malicious Code. “Malicious
elements.label: text

elements.text: Code” means software or firmware intended to perform an unauthorized process that
elements.label: text

elements.text: may have adverse impacts on the confidentiality, integrity, or availability of an
elements.label: text

elements.text: information system (including, without limitation, data in transit), such as a “virus,”
elements.label: text

elements.text: “time bomb,” “worm,” “trojan horse,” or other code-based entity that infects a host;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ransomware, spyware and certain forms of adware are also examples of Malicious
elements.label: text

elements.text: Code.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Conduct regular audits to ensure compliance with data loss prevention policies and
elements.label: text

elements.text: procedures and verify that data loss prevention software is functioning as intended
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For data loss prevention policies, implement a data classification scheme to identify
elements.label: text

elements.text: and categorize Freddie Mac confidential information and personal information
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure adequate and up-to-date data loss prevention software is used and a
elements.label: text

elements.text: corresponding management process is in place to scan all inbound files and e-mails
elements.label: text

elements.text: for malware
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Establish an incident response plan for data loss prevention policy violations. Ensure
elements.label: text

elements.text: staff are trained to respond to potential data loss incidents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vi)Anti-virus program/updates
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must install anti-virus software to protect servers and end user systems
elements.label: text

elements.text: and must keep all such software up to date with the latest anti-virus software and
elements.label: text

elements.text: definitions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vii) Network security
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Implement information technology controls such as stateful firewalls to block all
elements.label: text

elements.text: traffic inbound from, and outbound to, public networks that have not been expressly
elements.label: text

elements.text: permitted by policy (i.e., “deny by default”)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Manage and restrict ports, protocols and services to only those that are required and
elements.label: text

elements.text: approved for business operations
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Formally recertify and authorize firewall rules upon each significant change
elements.label: text

elements.text: (including, but not limited to, physical appliance updates, firmware updates and
elements.label: text

elements.text: other changes to firewall technology) in infrastructure and otherwise not less than
elements.label: text

elements.text: annually
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Define a network segmentation strategy, commensurate with the Seller/Servicer’s
