elements.label: text

elements.text: risk profile, that is documented in policies and procedures and requires physical and
elements.label: text

elements.text: logical segmentation from the user environment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Establish a comprehensive strategy and process for endpoint detection and response
elements.label: text

elements.text: that includes continuous monitoring of endpoint detection and response and remote
elements.label: text

elements.text: access technologies to detect any misuse or abuse
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ At least annually, review and update the endpoint detection and response strategy
elements.label: text

elements.text: and processes to adapt to evolving threats and technologies
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Define criteria and indicators to identify when privileged credentials are
elements.label: text

elements.text: compromised or used maliciously
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Develop procedures for prompt response and mitigation of identified threats,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ensuring that all incidents involving compromised credentials are thoroughly
elements.label: text

elements.text: investigated and remediated
elements.label: text

elements.text: (viii) Privacy policy
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must maintain a written privacy policy that has been approved by
elements.label: text

elements.text: management and communicated to all appropriate personnel. The policy must meet
elements.label: text

elements.text: industry best practices and require the Seller/Servicer to have an easily accessible
elements.label: text

elements.text: online privacy notice that complies with applicable laws.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ix) Mobile computing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must maintain a written mobile device/computing management
elements.label: text

elements.text: (MDM) policy that has been approved by management and communicated to all
elements.label: text

elements.text: appropriate personnel. This policy must reflect current and best practices, specifying
elements.label: text

elements.text: parameters including but not limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Approved and prohibited applications
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cryptographic mechanisms to ensure data security
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Identity and access management requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Software updates
elements.label: text

elements.text: (x) Wireless networks
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must control, secure, and monitor wireless access points. In addition,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers that offer wireless networks for network users must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Implement and keep up to date a strong Wireless Local Area Network (WLAN)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Authentication method that meets or exceeds the current industry standard Encryption
elements.label: text

elements.text: strength and technology
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Prohibit use of outdated wireless technologies such as Wired Equivalent Privacy
elements.label: text

elements.text: (WEP)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ At least annually, perform reviews of approved wireless networks to validate and
elements.label: text

elements.text: verify authorized users and access points
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Password protect and control administrative access to the router
elements.label: text

elements.text: (xi)Vulnerability management and penetration testing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must conduct vulnerability testing on a regular basis and have a process
elements.label: text

elements.text: in place to analyze and remediate identified vulnerabilities. To accomplish this, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Not less than annually, employ a qualified and independent third party to conduct
elements.label: text

elements.text: penetration testing on systems or system components used to store, access, process
elements.label: text

elements.text: and/or transmit Freddie Mac confidential information or Protected Information or
elements.label: text

elements.text: connect to System(s). At a minimum, the executive summary of the penetration report
elements.label: text

elements.text: on Freddie Mac-related services and data should be made available to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: for review.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maintain a written vulnerability assessment process and policy that has been
elements.label: text

elements.text: approved by Senior Management, including, at a minimum, the Chief Information
elements.label: text

elements.text: Officer, Chief Technology Officer, Chief Information Security Office or Chief Risk
elements.label: text

elements.text: Officer (or the equivalents thereof), communicated to appropriate personnel and has
elements.label: text

elements.text: an owner that implements, maintains and reviews the policy at least annually to
elements.label: text

elements.text: ensure that it consistently reflects industry best practices
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ As needed, document a remediation plan and remediate all identified vulnerabilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: per defined service level agreements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maintain a record of all identified vulnerabilities and their remediation status
elements.label: text

elements.text: (xii) Configuration and patch management
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Implement and maintain a written patch management process and a policy that has
elements.label: text

elements.text: been approved by management, communicated to all appropriate personnel and has
elements.label: text

elements.text: a designated owner that reviews, implements and maintains the policy to ensure that
elements.label: text

elements.text: it consistently reflects industry best practices
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Develop and execute a process for developing and maintaining secure configuration
elements.label: text

elements.text: baselines (also known as hardening guides, baseline secure configurations) of
elements.label: text

elements.text: infrastructure components
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Deploy intrusion detection and/or prevention systems (IDS and/or IPS) with
elements.label: text

elements.text: generated events fed into centralized systems for analysis
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Define, implement and maintain preventive controls designed to block malicious
elements.label: text

elements.text: messages and attachments from entering the environment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Designate qualified personnel responsible for performing timely software updates
elements.label: text

elements.text: and patches and maintain a process for testing and installing software updates as
elements.label: text

elements.text: they become available
elements.label: text

elements.text: (xiii) Auditing, logging and monitoring
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Develop, implement and maintain written guidelines and requirements for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: logging and monitoring of activities and action within information systems. If the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer uses an enterprise log management function, the subject
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements must be integrated with such log management function. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer may elect to use an external vendor for information security
elements.label: text

elements.text: monitoring, subject to the provisions of this Chapter 1302.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Develop, implement and maintain written log retention and handling requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: to ensure logs retain relevant, useable and timely information sufficient to identify
elements.label: text

elements.text: user access and/or system activities
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Perform an independent security assessment of the control environment not less
elements.label: text

elements.text: than annually and upon the occurrence of any Incident (as defined in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: (xiv) Software and application development life cycle (SDLC)
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Seller/Servicer develops applications or software that store, access, process or
elements.label: text

elements.text: transmit Freddie Mac confidential information, Protected Information or connects to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Systems, the Seller/Servicer must develop, implement and maintain a written SDLC
elements.label: text

elements.text: process and policy that has been approved by management. This policy must include at
elements.label: text

elements.text: minimum:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Management and separation of production and development environments that
elements.label: text

elements.text: reflect contemporary best practices
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Secure coding requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Open-source requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Code development and scanning pre- and post-deployment
elements.label: text

elements.text: (xv) Data Encryption
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maintain a formal Encryption and cryptography use policy that has been approved
elements.label: text

elements.text: by Senior Management, including, at a minimum, the Chief Information Officer,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chief Technology Officer, Chief Information Security Office or Chief Risk Officer
elements.label: text

elements.text: (or the equivalents thereof), and which has been communicated to appropriate
elements.label: text

elements.text: personnel and has an owner that implements, maintains and reviews the policy to
elements.label: text

elements.text: ensure it consistently reflects industry best practices
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maintain an encryption solution that enables the recovery of a compromised
elements.label: text

elements.text: database administrator account
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maintain encryption solutions that allow privileged administrators to complete
elements.label: text

elements.text: required actions without the ability to decrypt data
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure the protection, integrity and confidentiality of Freddie Mac confidential
elements.label: text

elements.text: information and Protected Information using encryption methods while in transit
elements.label: text

elements.text: and at rest
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Deploy cryptography standards that meet or exceed the then-current industry
elements.label: text

elements.text: standard Encryption strength and technology and prohibit use of outdated
elements.label: text

elements.text: technologies
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Generate, exchange, store, use, replace and delete cryptographic keys in a timely
elements.label: text

elements.text: manner to prevent unauthorized access to those keys
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Use Encryption mechanisms on portable end-user devices to protect data if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: hardware (laptop, mobile device, etc.) is lost or stolen
elements.label: text

elements.text: (xvi) Incident management
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Develop and maintain, and implement when triggered, an incident response plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: that provides a roadmap for implementing incident response capabilities and defines
elements.label: text

elements.text: the resources and management support needed. The plan must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Be approved by Senior Management, including, at a minimum, the Chief
elements.label: text

elements.text: Information Officer, Chief Technology Officer, Chief Information Security
elements.label: text

elements.text: Office or Chief Risk Officer (or the equivalents thereof);
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Include a plan, with a clearly defined process, to shut off access to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Systems when an Incident occurs;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ As specified in Section 1302.5, address any security breaches or incidents
elements.label: text

elements.text: involving Freddie Mac confidential information or Protected Information
elements.label: text

elements.text: promptly and effectively;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Be tested at a pre-defined periodic frequency, or more frequently, if prudent,
elements.label: text

elements.text: given the circumstances;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Be reviewed and updated at least annually
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Document a process to identify and respond to malicious domains, taking
elements.label: text

elements.text: immediate action to block access to these domains across all network and endpoint
elements.label: text

elements.text: security systems
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Notify relevant stakeholders and provide detailed reports on the identified threats
elements.label: text

elements.text: and actions taken
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Coordinate with Related Third Parties on Incident detection and remediation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Regularly review and update the response processes to adapt to evolving threats and
elements.label: text

elements.text: technologies, incorporating lessons learned from past incidents to enhance the
elements.label: text

elements.text: overall effectiveness of the response strategy
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Annually, unless formally activated, test the effectiveness of the incident response
elements.label: text

elements.text: plan and capabilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Annually, unless formally activated, audit the incident response plan. The audit may
elements.label: text

elements.text: be performed by (i) an internal independent function within the organization, or (ii)
elements.label: text

elements.text: an external entity who is qualified to do such audits;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Evaluate lessons learned from all Incidents;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Implement or identify an existing classification scale for Incidents to quantify the
elements.label: text

elements.text: severity of the Incident;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have documented action plans for remediation of Incidents, including playbooks
elements.label: text

elements.text: for Incidents related to AI/ML (as defined in Section 1302.8)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (xvii)Access control
elements.label: text

elements.text: A. Access management policy
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller/Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Establish, implement and maintain an access management policy that aligns
elements.label: text

elements.text: with industry best practices, including a process for granting and removing
elements.label: text

elements.text: system access, requirements for Authentication and rules of behavior. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: access management policy must be reviewed and updated at least annually.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Define a process for securing administrative user and other privileged accounts,
elements.label: text

elements.text: which must be reviewed and updated at least annually, and enforce access and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Authentication requirements, including establishing and maintaining multifactor
elements.label: text

elements.text: authentication procedures for system administrators and other privileged
elements.label: text

elements.text: accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Define and enforce remote access requirements, including acceptable use,
elements.label: text

elements.text: approvals and recertification processes
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Define and enforce requirements around locked accounts after multiple failed
elements.label: text

elements.text: login attempts and timeout requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Establish and enforce access control methods that limit access to systems,
elements.label: text

elements.text: physical or virtual resources and grant access to users, including third parties,
elements.label: text

elements.text: on a need-to-know basis
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Define and enforce requirements for multifactor authentication where applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: (privileged sessions, remote connectivity, applications housing Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: confidential information or Protected Information, etc.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Manage user accounts for System(s), in accordance with the Guide and the other
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Documents. Seller/Servicers must monitor account access for users
elements.label: text

elements.text: who transfer roles or are terminated or no longer need access to their accounts.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must notify Freddie Mac (see Directory 8) within one Business
elements.label: text

elements.text: Day after any transfer or termination.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to and comply with the instructions to update systems access for relevant
elements.label: text

elements.text: applications at https://sf.freddiemac.com/tools-learning/technology-login
elements.label: text

elements.text: B. Granting, removing and reviewing access
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must maintain and enforce written procedures to ensure that only
elements.label: text

elements.text: necessary personnel are granted access to Freddie Mac Systems. Access should be
elements.label: text

elements.text: granted based on the principle of least privilege, ensuring that individuals have only
elements.label: text

elements.text: the access necessary for their roles. Access to sensitive information is limited to
elements.label: text

elements.text: authorized users only, commensurate with their roles and responsibilities, on a
elements.label: text

elements.text: need-to-know basis. The procedures must specifically address:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Approval of access requests
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Removal of access for terminations and transfers
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Analysis of user access and removal of access that is inactive or no longer
elements.label: text

elements.text: needed
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ At least annual review of all user access privileges and certification of access
elements.label: text

elements.text: according to the minimum information necessary to access permission rules
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ At least twice annual review of all administrator roles and responsibilities and
elements.label: text

elements.text: certification of access according to the minimum necessary to access permission
elements.label: text

elements.text: rules
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Timely compliance with Freddie Mac requests pertaining to recertification of
elements.label: text

elements.text: user access privileges and/or administrator roles for Systems
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Prohibit or prevent using the same service account identifiers and passwords in
elements.label: text

elements.text: both production and non-production environments
elements.label: text

elements.text: C. Authentication requirements and guidelines
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must authenticate employee identity with a multifactor
elements.label: text

elements.text: authentication method or process that includes, but is not limited to, user
elements.label: text

elements.text: identification codes, passwords, personal identification numbers, a smart card
elements.label: text

elements.text: and/or a token device. Ensure that this process is fatigue resistant. Ensure that all
elements.label: text

elements.text: access to Freddie Mac data requires multifactor authentication.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers should establish comprehensive procedures to monitor all
elements.label: text

elements.text: enterprise Authentication services and processes. This includes defining criteria and
elements.label: text

elements.text: indicators for detecting credential compromise or anomalous activity. Regular
elements.label: text

elements.text: monitoring and analysis should be conducted to identify and respond to potential
elements.label: text

elements.text: threats promptly. Additionally, third-party access should be continuously reviewed
elements.label: text

elements.text: and updated to ensure compliance with security policies and to mitigate risks.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If passwords are used, the Authentication policy must mandate, and Seller/Servicers
elements.label: text

elements.text: must enforce, minimum guidelines for password complexity, reuse timelines and
elements.label: text

elements.text: password change timelines.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must utilize password vaults with multifactor authentication and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ensure that no passwords are stored in unsecured files.
elements.label: text

elements.text: D. Asset management
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must implement security measures to protect Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: confidential information and Protected Information, including encryption, secure
elements.label: text

elements.text: storage solutions and regular security assessments. Seller/Servicers must ensure
elements.label: text

elements.text: compliance with all relevant regulations and standards for data protection and
elements.label: text

elements.text: confidentiality.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must maintain an inventory management system to track physical
elements.label: text

elements.text: and software assets, such as end-user technology, servers, network devices, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: corresponding asset ownership. The inventory management system must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: reconciled to actual inventory at least annually to verify all assets are included.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must develop procedures for continuous monitoring of access and
elements.label: text

elements.text: usage of Freddie Mac confidential information and Protected Information to detect
elements.label: text

elements.text: and respond to any unauthorized access or anomalies.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documented procedures must be in place detailing guidelines and requirements for
elements.label: text

elements.text: tracking the removal of assets from a facility.
elements.label: text

elements.text: E. Cloud computing
elements.label: text

elements.text: When a Seller/Servicer consumes or provides cloud services that store, process,
elements.label: text

elements.text: access or transmit Freddie Mac confidential information or Protected Information or
elements.label: text

elements.text: connect to any System, the Seller/Servicer must maintain and comply with policies
elements.label: text

elements.text: and standards for managing cloud computing risks. The policy should address:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Due Diligence: Specify appropriate due diligence responsibilities, governance,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ongoing oversight and monitoring of the cloud service providers’ security
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ System vulnerabilities: Articulate processes and responsibilities to securely
elements.label: text

elements.text: configure cloud systems, provision access, and log and monitor the FM
elements.label: text

elements.text: information assets residing in or being processed in the cloud environment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Create and maintain a process for determining the criteria for replacing or
elements.label: text

elements.text: remediating the services provided by a cloud service provider when it has
elements.label: text

elements.text: suffered an incident.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Identity and access management: Define roles for cloud access management,
elements.label: text

elements.text: limiting account privileges, implementing multifactor authentication, frequently
elements.label: text

elements.text: updating and reviewing account access, monitoring activity, and requiring
elements.label: text

elements.text: privileged users to have separate usernames and passwords
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Security controls for sensitive data: Define responsibilities for implementing
elements.label: text

elements.text: controls to safeguard sensitive data limit a malicious actor’s ability to exploit
elements.label: text

elements.text: data during a breach
elements.label: text

elements.text: The cloud policy should be approved by management and communicated to
elements.label: text

elements.text: appropriate personnel, and the Seller/Servicer must designate an owner to maintain
elements.label: text

elements.text: and review the policy to ensure it consistently reflects industry best practices.
elements.label: text

elements.text: F. Vendor risk management program
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must implement a vendor risk management program to formally
elements.label: text

elements.text: evaluate, track and measure third-party risk; to assess its impact on all aspects of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: organization’s business; and to develop compensating controls or other forms of
elements.label: text

elements.text: mitigation to safeguard and protect Freddie Mac confidential information, Protected
elements.label: text

elements.text: Information and Systems from unauthorized persons, malicious software or other
elements.label: text

elements.text: harmful computer information, commands, codes or programs. The risk
elements.label: text

elements.text: management program must include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: I. Criteria for determining when to replace a Related Third Party that has
elements.label: text

elements.text: experienced or is experiencing an incident and
elements.label: text

elements.text: II. A process to replace the Related Third Party when such criteria are met
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must maintain with all Related Third Parties that store, process,
elements.label: text

elements.text: access or transmit Freddie Mac confidential information or Protected Information a
elements.label: text

elements.text: written agreement that obligates them to comply with Minimum Requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: similar to what is outlined within this chapter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1302.2: Information security (Future effective date
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Defined terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Information security minimum requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Defined terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers should be familiar with the following defined terms as they relate to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of Section 1302.2:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Information security defined terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: A
elements.label: text

elements.text: Authentication The process in which a system verifies the identity and role of an
elements.label: text

elements.text: individual, usually based on some form of credential(s)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (password/ID, token, etc.).
elements.label: text

elements.text: E
elements.label: text

elements.text: Encryption The process of encoding or obfuscating messages or information in
elements.label: text

elements.text: such a way that only authorized parties can read it.
elements.label: text

elements.text: F
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Critical Data
elements.label: text

elements.text: All information necessary for the Seller/Servicer to perform its
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligations under the Seller/Servicer’s Purchase Documents,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including, but not limited to, Freddie Mac confidential information
elements.label: text

elements.text: and Protected Information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: V
elements.label: text

elements.text: Vulnerability
elements.label: text

elements.text: Management
elements.label: text

elements.text: The process of identifying and testing known software
elements.label: text

elements.text: vulnerabilities within a system and prioritizing remediation
elements.label: text

elements.text: according to each vulnerability’s likelihood of occurrence and how
elements.label: text

elements.text: the exploitation of the vulnerability would impact the system.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Information security minimum requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Information security program
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must define an individual or group of individuals responsible for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: development of information security requirements, including the adoption,
elements.label: text

elements.text: implementation, maintenance and administration of written minimum-security standards,
elements.label: text

elements.text: policies and procedures that responsibly address critical issues such as user
elements.label: text

elements.text: responsibilities (e.g., “Acceptable Use”); ownership of and access to information;
elements.label: text

elements.text: baseline security practices; physical, administrative and technical security protection
elements.label: text

elements.text: mechanisms and other requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Not less than annually, Seller/Servicers must review and assess the adequacy of their
elements.label: text

elements.text: information security policies and procedures used in connection with the selling and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing of Freddie Mac Mortgages to ensure compliance with the Guide, their other
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Documents and industry best practices (including as set forth by the National
elements.label: text

elements.text: Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) and International Organization for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Standardization (ISO)/International Electrotechnical Commission (IEC) standards).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon request of Freddie Mac, Seller/Servicers must make their information security
elements.label: text

elements.text: program policies and procedures available; further, upon request of Freddie Mac,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must provide an attestation executed by a duly authorized corporate
elements.label: text

elements.text: officer of the adequacy of these policies and procedures, including following the
elements.label: text

elements.text: termination of a Seller/Servicer’s right to sell or service Mortgages, or the occurrence of
elements.label: text

elements.text: an Incident (as defined in Section 1302.5).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Human resources security
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following are requirements related to human resources security:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Pre-employment screening: Seller/Servicers must conduct, or retain a qualified third
elements.label: text

elements.text: party to conduct, thorough background verification checks (screening) for all
elements.label: text

elements.text: candidates for employment or contractor status who will have access to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: confidential information, Protected Information or Systems (as defined in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Code of conduct or non-disclosure agreement: Prior to being granted access to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac confidential information, Protected Information or Systems,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must require all employees, contractors and third parties to (i) sign a
elements.label: text

elements.text: non-disclosure agreement or (ii) be subject to a code of conduct, which in either case
elements.label: text

elements.text: includes obligations to restrict the use or disclosure of and to maintain as confidential
elements.label: text

elements.text: all Freddie Mac confidential information
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Protected Information and information related to or contained in Systems: The code
elements.label: text

elements.text: of conduct must be acknowledged by the employee, contractor or third party, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: must address at least the following subjects:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Appropriate use of company assets
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Information protection, including non-disclosure and confidentiality
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Records management
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Information security and privacy
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Business courtesies
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Personal investments and insider trading
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Conflicts of interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Information security awareness, education and training: At least annually,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must provide information security awareness training to all
elements.label: text

elements.text: employees and contractors who have access to Freddie Mac confidential information,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Protected Information and/or Systems.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The training should incorporate current cybersecurity threats, including phishing,
elements.label: text

elements.text: social engineering, supply chain attacks, malware/ransomware, credential
elements.label: text

elements.text: compromise via weak password hygiene, insider threats, artificial intelligence (AI)-
elements.label: text

elements.text: powered tactics (e.g., deepfakes, targeted phishing content) and threats to AI systems
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e.g., model inversion, data poisoning, prompt injection). When necessary, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: organization personnel and partners must receive AI risk management training to
elements.label: text

elements.text: enable them to perform their duties and responsibilities consistent with related
elements.label: text

elements.text: policies, procedures and agreements. At a minimum, the training must address the
elements.label: text

elements.text: importance of data security, provide details on roles and responsibilities for all users
elements.label: text

elements.text: in protecting information at the Seller/Servicer (along with practical ways to
elements.label: text

elements.text: incorporate information security into daily routines) and include the specific
elements.label: text

elements.text: processes in place for protecting Freddie Mac confidential information and Protected
elements.label: text

elements.text: Information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Physical and environmental security controls
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must create and maintain:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A physical security control program of the organization’s buildings and facilities that
elements.label: text

elements.text: contain information systems, designed to detect, monitor and prevent unauthorized
elements.label: text

elements.text: access and to respond to physical security incidents using real-time physical intrusion
elements.label: text

elements.text: alarms and surveillance equipment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An updated list of personnel with authorized access to facilities where information
elements.label: text

elements.text: systems reside, including an access privilege review performed not less than annually
elements.label: text

elements.text: and upon the departure of any authorized personnel
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Environmental controls to monitor, mitigate and protect the organization with regards
elements.label: text

elements.text: to a loss of connectivity, access to or integrity of information and damage caused by
elements.label: text

elements.text: natural disasters or man-made incidents such as fire, earthquake, flood, hurricane,
elements.label: text

elements.text: tornado or weather-related adverse conditions

elements.text: promptly and effectively;

elements.text: given the circumstances;

elements.text: security systems

elements.text: and actions taken

elements.text: overall effectiveness of the response strategy

elements.text: plan and capabilities

elements.text: (xvii)Access control
elements.label: text

elements.text: A. Access management policy
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller/Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Establish, implement and maintain an access management policy that aligns
elements.label: text

elements.text: with industry best practices, including a process for granting and removing
elements.label: text

elements.text: system access, requirements for Authentication and rules of behavior. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: access management policy must be reviewed and updated at least annually.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Define a process for securing administrative user and other privileged accounts,
elements.label: text

elements.text: which must be reviewed and updated at least annually, and enforce access and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Authentication requirements, including establishing and maintaining multifactor
elements.label: text

elements.text: authentication procedures for system administrators and other privileged
elements.label: text

elements.text: accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Define and enforce remote access requirements, including acceptable use,
elements.label: text

elements.text: approvals and recertification processes
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Define and enforce requirements around locked accounts after multiple failed
elements.label: text

elements.text: login attempts and timeout requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Establish and enforce access control methods that limit access to systems,
elements.label: text

elements.text: physical or virtual resources and grant access to users, including third parties,
elements.label: text

elements.text: on a need-to-know basis
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Define and enforce requirements for multifactor authentication where applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: (privileged sessions, remote connectivity, applications housing Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: confidential information or Protected Information, etc.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Manage user accounts for System(s), in accordance with the Guide and the other
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Documents. Seller/Servicers must monitor account access for users
elements.label: text

elements.text: who transfer roles or are terminated or no longer need access to their accounts.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must notify Freddie Mac (see Directory 8) within one Business
elements.label: text

elements.text: Day after any transfer or termination.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to and comply with the instructions to update systems access for relevant
elements.label: text

elements.text: applications at https://sf.freddiemac.com/tools-learning/technology-login
elements.label: text

elements.text: B. Granting, removing and reviewing access
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must maintain and enforce written procedures to ensure that only
elements.label: text

elements.text: necessary personnel are granted access to Freddie Mac Systems. Access should be
elements.label: text

elements.text: granted based on the principle of least privilege, ensuring that individuals have only
elements.label: text

elements.text: the access necessary for their roles. Access to sensitive information is limited to
elements.label: text

elements.text: authorized users only, commensurate with their roles and responsibilities, on a
elements.label: text

elements.text: need-to-know basis. The procedures must specifically address:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Approval of access requests
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Removal of access for terminations and transfers
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Analysis of user access and removal of access that is inactive or no longer
elements.label: text

elements.text: needed
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ At least annual review of all user access privileges and certification of access
elements.label: text

elements.text: according to the minimum information necessary to access permission rules
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ At least twice annual review of all administrator roles and responsibilities and
elements.label: text

elements.text: certification of access according to the minimum necessary to access permission
elements.label: text

elements.text: rules
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Timely compliance with Freddie Mac requests pertaining to recertification of
elements.label: text

elements.text: user access privileges and/or administrator roles for Systems
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Prohibit or prevent using the same service account identifiers and passwords in
elements.label: text

elements.text: both production and non-production environments
elements.label: text

elements.text: C. Authentication requirements and guidelines
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must authenticate employee identity with a multifactor
elements.label: text

elements.text: authentication method or process that includes, but is not limited to, user
elements.label: text

elements.text: identification codes, passwords, personal identification numbers, a smart card
elements.label: text

elements.text: and/or a token device. Ensure that this process is fatigue resistant. Ensure that all
elements.label: text

elements.text: access to Freddie Mac data requires multifactor authentication.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers should establish comprehensive procedures to monitor all
elements.label: text

elements.text: enterprise Authentication services and processes. This includes defining criteria and
elements.label: text

elements.text: indicators for detecting credential compromise or anomalous activity. Regular
elements.label: text

elements.text: monitoring and analysis should be conducted to identify and respond to potential
elements.label: text

elements.text: threats promptly. Additionally, third-party access should be continuously reviewed
elements.label: text

elements.text: and updated to ensure compliance with security policies and to mitigate risks.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If passwords are used, the Authentication policy must mandate, and Seller/Servicers
elements.label: text

elements.text: must enforce, minimum guidelines for password complexity, reuse timelines and
elements.label: text

elements.text: password change timelines.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must utilize password vaults with multifactor authentication and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ensure that no passwords are stored in unsecured files.
elements.label: text

elements.text: D. Asset management
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must implement security measures to protect Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: confidential information and Protected Information, including encryption, secure
elements.label: text

elements.text: storage solutions and regular security assessments. Seller/Servicers must ensure
elements.label: text

elements.text: compliance with all relevant regulations and standards for data protection and
elements.label: text

elements.text: confidentiality.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must maintain an inventory management system to track physical
elements.label: text

elements.text: and software assets, such as end-user technology, servers, network devices, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: corresponding asset ownership. The inventory management system must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: reconciled to actual inventory at least annually to verify all assets are included.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must develop procedures for continuous monitoring of access and
elements.label: text

elements.text: usage of Freddie Mac confidential information and Protected Information to detect
elements.label: text

elements.text: and respond to any unauthorized access or anomalies.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documented procedures must be in place detailing guidelines and requirements for
elements.label: text

elements.text: tracking the removal of assets from a facility.
elements.label: text

elements.text: E. Cloud computing
elements.label: text

elements.text: When a Seller/Servicer consumes or provides cloud services that store, process,
elements.label: text

elements.text: access or transmit Freddie Mac confidential information or Protected Information or
elements.label: text

elements.text: connect to any System, the Seller/Servicer must maintain and comply with policies
elements.label: text

elements.text: and standards for managing cloud computing risks. The policy should address:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Due Diligence: Specify appropriate due diligence responsibilities, governance,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ongoing oversight and monitoring of the cloud service providers’ security
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ System vulnerabilities: Articulate processes and responsibilities to securely
elements.label: text

elements.text: configure cloud systems, provision access, and log and monitor the FM
elements.label: text

elements.text: information assets residing in or being processed in the cloud environment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Create and maintain a process for determining the criteria for replacing or
elements.label: text

elements.text: remediating the services provided by a cloud service provider when it has
elements.label: text

elements.text: suffered an incident.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Identity and access management: Define roles for cloud access management,
elements.label: text

elements.text: limiting account privileges, implementing multifactor authentication, frequently
elements.label: text

elements.text: updating and reviewing account access, monitoring activity, and requiring
elements.label: text

elements.text: privileged users to have separate usernames and passwords
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Security controls for sensitive data: Define responsibilities for implementing
elements.label: text

elements.text: controls to safeguard sensitive data limit a malicious actor’s ability to exploit
elements.label: text

elements.text: data during a breach
elements.label: text

elements.text: The cloud policy should be approved by management and communicated to
elements.label: text

elements.text: appropriate personnel, and the Seller/Servicer must designate an owner to maintain
elements.label: text

elements.text: and review the policy to ensure it consistently reflects industry best practices.
elements.label: text

elements.text: F. Vendor risk management program
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must implement a vendor risk management program to formally
elements.label: text

elements.text: evaluate, track and measure third-party risk; to assess its impact on all aspects of the
elements.label: text
